TITLE: l'enseignement technique et professionnel, adoptée par
paragraph: Noting that the gradual blurring of the boundaries between manual labor and intellectual work constitutes a positive trend,

low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph references evolving labor boundaries as a positive trend, aligning with 'Life skills and 21st century competencies' as it relates to adapting to societal changes. 'Other cross-cutting theme' is included due to the debate's uncertainty about categorization. Categories like digital literacy or economic literacy are excluded as the text does not specifically mention skills related to technology or finance.

TITLE: l'enseignement technique et professionnel, adoptée par
paragraph: Aware that the forms which interaction between education and productive work may take vary from one country to another,

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph mentions varying forms of education-work interaction across countries, aligning with governance and planning (frameworks for systemic approaches). 'Other policy theme' is included due to the lack of a precise fit among existing categories. Categories like 'Education orientation' and 'Cross-cutting themes' are excluded as the text focuses on structural variations rather than systemic approaches or adaptable skills.
low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph mentions varying forms of education-work interaction, implying both academic (general) and vocational/technical (TVET) pathways. 'Combined general and vocational' is excluded as the text does not explicitly endorse integration, only acknowledges diversity.

TITLE: l'enseignement technique et professionnel, adoptée par
paragraph: Considering that, given the rapid evolution taking place in the field of work, it is essential that training in new technology sectors be introduced simultaneously with the development of knowledge in traditional sectors, and that lifelong education provide young people and adults with opportunities to regularly update and improve their theoretical and practical knowledge in accordance with scientific, technical, economic, cultural, and social progress,

low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes integrating new technology sectors with traditional sectors and lifelong learning, which aligns with 'Combined general and vocational' as it addresses both academic and technical aspects. 'General (academic)' and 'Vocational/technical (TVET)' are excluded because the text explicitly mentions dual sector integration, requiring a combined approach.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes new technology sectors and lifelong learning, aligning with 'Digital and ICT literacy'. 'Life skills and 21st century competencies' fits due to the focus on continuous skill development and adaptation. Other categories like 'Human rights' or 'Sustainable development' are not explicitly mentioned, and 'Entrepreneurship' lacks direct reference.

TITLE: l'enseignement technique et professionnel, adoptée par
paragraph: Considering further that it clearly emerges from the recommendations of the latest regional conferences of ministers of education organized by UNESCO that one of the most urgent needs of today's societies, particularly developing societies, is to produce a skilled workforce adapted to the employment situation and meeting the requirements of development, these being notably defined in light of the national economic and technical development plans and programs of the societies in question,

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes aligning education with national development plans and workforce needs, directly linking to governance and planning. The high-level debate's mention of 'strategic alignment' and 'policy-driven frameworks' supports 'Policy theme'. Other categories like 'Curriculum' or 'Skills' are less directly addressed here.

TITLE: l'enseignement technique et professionnel, adoptée par
paragraph: Noting furthermore that developing countries, in particular, are far from having the resources necessary to meet the most legitimate demands aimed at ensuring the interaction between school, productive work, and the world of employment, and that many developing countries are concerned about the inadequacy of infrastructures resulting from the lack of resources needed to acquire the simplest tools and equipment, to construct facilities suitable for practical activities, or to guarantee a steady supply of essential materials for the implementation of the most rudimentary forms of productive work,

low_level_thinking.Infrastructure & resources: The paragraph explicitly mentions inadequate infrastructures for practical activities, lack of resources for tools/equipment, and insufficient supply of essential materials, aligning with 'School buildings and physical infrastructure' and 'Teaching and learning materials'. 'Transport and boarding' is included due to the mention of 'steady supply of essential materials' implying logistical challenges. 'ICT and digital infrastructure' and 'WASH facilities' are not directly mentioned. 'Learner population' is a broader debate point but not a sub-label here.
low_level_thinking.Learner population: The paragraph specifically mentions developing countries lacking resources for education infrastructure, aligning with 'Low-income and disadvantaged groups'. It also references inadequate facilities and materials, which supports 'Rural and remote learners' facing resource limitations. Other categories like 'Children (0-17)' or 'Youth and adolescents' are not explicitly mentioned, and 'Population in conflict' is not referenced.

TITLE: l'enseignement technique et professionnel, adoptée par
paragraph: Taking into account that there is a link between the right to education and the right to work, and recognizing that education must, in all societies, contribute effectively to material and cultural well-being as well as to socio-economic and cultural development,

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph links education to socio-economic and cultural development, aligning with 'Access and equity' through its focus on effective contribution to well-being. 'Sustainable development and environment' is relevant due to the mention of socio-economic and cultural development as part of broader sustainable progress. Other categories like 'Policy theme' and 'Cross-cutting themes & skills' are excluded as the paragraph does not explicitly address legal frameworks or interdisciplinary skills.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph links education to socio-economic development and well-being, aligning with 'Sustainable development and climate action' (development focus). The 'Human rights and equality' category fits due to the mention of rights (education and work). 'Life skills and 21st century competencies' matches the emphasis on education's role in contributing to socio-economic development, which includes skills for modern society. 'Global citizenship and peace education' and 'Entrepreneurship and business skills' are less directly supported, while 'Digital and ICT literacy' and 'Financial and economic literacy' are not explicitly mentioned.

TITLE: l'enseignement technique et professionnel, adoptée par
paragraph: Considering that the success of harmonious development in any society is fostered by the mobilization of all human resources, without distinction of gender, primarily involving the genuine promotion of equal opportunities in relation to education and technical and vocational training, which are factors of well-being, cultural, social, technological, and economic progress,

low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph explicitly mentions both education and technical/vocational training as factors for societal progress, supporting both categories. 'Combined general and vocational' is excluded as the paragraph does not explicitly frame them as integrated. 'General (academic)' and 'Vocational/technical (TVET)' are directly referenced, aligning with the orchestrator's emphasis on equal opportunities in education and training.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes equal opportunities in education and training, aligning with 'Human rights and equality'. It also mentions skills development (education, technical, vocational) as factors for progress, fitting 'Life skills and 21st century competencies'. 'Other cross-cutting theme' was excluded as the focus is explicitly on education-related skills, not broader societal themes.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes equal opportunities in education and training, aligning with 'Access and equity' and 'Inclusion and diversity' categories. 'Education orientation' and 'Cross-cutting themes & skills' are not direct matches, while other categories like 'Curriculum' or 'Pedagogy' are excluded as the focus is on equity, not specific educational methods or content.

TITLE: l'enseignement technique et professionnel, adoptée par
paragraph: Convinced that it is essential to further develop regional and international cooperation in the field of education to promote peace, mutual respect, and understanding among peoples,

low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes promoting peace, mutual respect, and understanding, aligning with 'Global citizenship and peace education'. 'Inclusive education and special needs' is included as the text mentions 'regional and international cooperation' which supports inclusive, collaborative education frameworks. Other categories like 'Policy theme' are excluded per the orchestrator's note that 'Cross-cutting themes & skills' is preferred over 'Policy theme'.
low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes cooperation in education to promote peace and mutual respect, aligning with general academic themes. Vocational/technical (TVET) is excluded as there's no mention of specific skills training. Combined general and vocational is not selected due to lack of explicit vocational focus.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph explicitly mentions 'regional and international cooperation' which directly maps to 'International cooperation and partnerships'. The reference to developing cooperation mechanisms and the debate context justify 'Policy theme' as a high-level label. Other categories like 'Inclusion and diversity' or 'Sustainable development' are not directly addressed in the paragraph.

TITLE: l'enseignement technique et professionnel, adoptée par
paragraph: And taking into account the constitutional provisions in force in the various Member States as well as their specific needs and educational systems,

low_level_thinking.Legal frameworks: The paragraph explicitly mentions 'constitutional provisions' which directly aligns with 'Constitution and fundamental rights'. 'Education laws and regulations' is supported by the reference to 'educational systems' and 'specific needs', indicating existing legal structures. 'Legal frameworks' is too broad and not a sub-category allowed. 'Stakeholder focus' is not a permitted category.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The mention of 'constitutional provisions' and 'specific needs' aligns with government and policy makers as they are responsible for legal frameworks and policy. 'Parents and communities' are referenced through 'specific needs' and 'educational systems,' indicating their role in shaping educational contexts. Other categories like 'Teachers and educators' or 'Civil society and NGOs' are not explicitly mentioned.

TITLE: l'enseignement technique et professionnel, adoptée par
paragraph: Adopts, on November 19, 1981, the following recommendation, which it submits to the ministries of education, other competent authorities and institutions of the Member States, as well as to other institutions of the United Nations system with responsibilities in this field, and to interested non-governmental organizations:

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph mentions submitting recommendations to multiple stakeholders including UN system institutions and NGOs, aligning with 'International cooperation and partnerships'. The reference to ministries and competent authorities relates to governance structures, supporting 'Governance and planning'. Other categories like access or quality are not explicitly addressed.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph explicitly mentions ministries of education, competent authorities, UN system institutions, and NGOs as recipients of the recommendation, aligning with Government and policy makers, International organizations and donors, and Civil society and NGOs. Learners, teachers, parents are not mentioned, so they are excluded.

TITLE: l'enseignement technique et professionnel, adoptée par
paragraph: 1. The interactions between education and productive work, the latter referring to material or intellectual goods or services useful to the individual or society without necessarily being remunerated, and taking into account the formative contribution of the productive sector itself, should be genuine and continuous,

low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph explicitly links education to productive work, which includes both material/intellectual contributions and formative roles, indicating a blend of general and vocational aspects. 'General' is excluded as the focus is not solely academic, and 'Vocational/technical' is insufficient since the reference to 'formative contribution of the productive sector' suggests broader integration beyond technical training.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes continuous interaction with productive work, which includes entrepreneurial and business skills (Entrepreneurship and business skills). It also mentions formative contributions of productive sectors, aligning with life skills needed for societal and individual development (Life skills and 21st century competencies). Other categories like digital literacy or human rights are not explicitly mentioned.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph mentions 'productive work' and the 'formative contribution of the productive sector', aligning with the private sector and industry. The reference to 'genuine and continuous' interactions suggests policy and systemic engagement, pointing to government and policy makers. Other categories like learners, parents, or civil society are not explicitly mentioned.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes systemic integration of education with productive work as a policy theme, aligning with the orchestrator's 'Policy theme' debate focus. Other categories like 'Inclusion and diversity' or 'Sustainable development' are not explicitly mentioned. 'Education orientation' and 'Cross-cutting themes' from the summary are broader concepts not directly mapped to the allowed categories.

TITLE: l'enseignement technique et professionnel, adoptée par
paragraph: 2. Efforts to ensure these interactions should be undertaken at all levels of education in all its forms and, while allowing for a certain independence of the elements involved, should emphasize the interdependence between theory and practice, the role and importance of productive work within social, aesthetic, cultural, economic, and individual values, and the best possible general and professional training.

low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph emphasizes the integration of theory and practice, productive work within societal values, and general/professional training, aligning with 'Content and learning areas' (structured educational content). 'Curriculum implementation' fits as it discusses how these elements should be undertaken across education levels. 'Other curriculum-related issue' was excluded due to lack of specific reference, and 'Education orientation' is not directly mentioned.
low_level_thinking.Pedagogy & assessment: The paragraph emphasizes interdependence between theory and practice, which aligns with pedagogical foundations and theories. It also mentions 'best possible general and professional training,' suggesting professional learning communities. 'Assessment and feedback' is excluded as the paragraph lacks specific mention of evaluation methods. 'Teaching methods and approaches' is too narrow, and 'Technology-supported pedagogy' is not referenced.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes interdependence between theory and practice, which aligns with life skills and 21st century competencies. Mention of social, cultural, economic values and productive work relates to sustainable development and climate action. The reference to 'all forms of education' and 'independence of elements' supports inclusive education and special needs.
low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes both theory-practice interdependence (general) and productive work within social, economic, and cultural values (vocational). 'Combined general and vocational' captures this dual focus. 'General' alone misses the vocational elements, while 'Vocational/technical' overlooks the theoretical and values-based aspects.

TITLE: l'enseignement technique et professionnel, adoptée par
paragraph: 3. Among the measures aimed at ensuring, strengthening, and promoting these interactions at various levels of education, there may be provisions intended to introduce or develop creative or productive activities within the curricula and participation in socially useful activities or in actual applied work inside or outside educational institutions,

low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph discusses introducing creative activities and socially useful work into curricula, aligning with 'Design and development' for curriculum creation. 'Curriculum implementation' fits as it mentions integrating these elements into educational settings. 'Content and learning areas' was excluded as the focus is on integration rather than specific content. 'Curriculum evaluation and reform' is not directly addressed.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes creative/productive activities, socially useful work, and applied learning, aligning with life skills and 21st century competencies. 'Sustainable development' is implied through 'socially useful activities' and 'applied work' contributing to societal well-being. 'Inclusive education' is suggested by 'various levels of education' and participation beyond institutions. Other categories like citizenship or digital literacy lack direct textual support.

TITLE: l'enseignement technique et professionnel, adoptée par
paragraph: 4. The interaction between education and productive work should be ensured mainly in two ways: (a) by introducing productive work into the educational process, in schools and other educational institutions; (b) by providing active individuals with more favorable conditions to continue their education without interrupting their professional activity.

low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph explicitly mentions integrating productive work into education (TVET) and supports continuous education during professional activity, aligning with vocational focus. 'Combined general and vocational' is chosen as the paragraph addresses both educational process (general) and professional activity (vocational). 'General (academic)' is excluded as the emphasis is on work-integrated learning rather than purely academic content.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes integrating productive work into education (curriculum/pedagogy) and continuous education without interrupting professional activity (lifelong learning). 'Productive work' aligns with competency-based skills in the summary, but 'Cross-cutting skills' is not a category. 'Governance' and 'Financing' are not directly addressed. 'Inclusion' and 'Equity' are not explicitly mentioned.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes integrating productive work into education (aligning with 'Life skills and 21st century competencies') and enabling continuous education during professional activity (linked to 'Entrepreneurship and business skills'). Other categories like 'Digital literacy' or 'Human rights' are not explicitly mentioned.
low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph emphasizes integrating productive work into education (Curriculum implementation) and aligns with competency-based skills mentioned in the orchestrator summary. 'Competency-based and learning outcomes' is supported by the focus on active individuals' professional development. 'Organization and instructional time' is excluded as the text doesn't specify scheduling, and 'Curriculum evaluation and reform' lacks mention of assessment or revision.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph emphasizes structural policy integration (Government and policy makers) and links education to productive work, which involves private sector and industry. Other categories like educators or learners are not explicitly mentioned, and the focus on policy and economic sectors aligns with the orchestrator's Stakeholder focus theme.
low_level_thinking.Learning modality: The paragraph mentions 'productive work into the educational process' (Formal education), 'providing active individuals with more favorable conditions to continue their education' (Lifelong learning), and 'schools and other educational institutions' (Formal education). 'Non-formal education' is included as 'productive work' may involve non-formal learning opportunities outside traditional schooling. 'Informal learning' is excluded as the text focuses on structured educational processes rather than unstructured learning.
low_level_thinking.Education system monitoring & evaluation: The paragraph discusses systemic integration of productive work into education and continuous learning, aligning with 'Education system monitoring & evaluation' as it involves structural oversight. 'Other' is included due to the unique focus on productive work and professional continuity, which doesn't fit neatly into existing categories.

TITLE: l'enseignement technique et professionnel, adoptée par
paragraph: 5. The activities related to the world of work that are included in the curriculum of educational institutions (concretely creative activities and productive work within schools or participation in socially useful activities and productive work outside this framework) should, as appropriate in each case and in accordance with national education structures: (a) correspond to the aims and objectives of education, be an integral part of the educational process, and seek to maintain an appropriate teaching framework or improve the factors determining this framework; (b) aim to ensure the balanced development of each individual’s physical, affective, and mental aptitudes, as well as their moral and aesthetic values, for their own benefit and that of society; (c) teach pupils and students to appreciate the economic and social value of different types of work and to instill through education respect for workers and the world of work in general, while developing each person’s ability to appropriately and freely choose their field of study, work, and career through guidance and counseling services within institutions and at the national level; (d) contribute to the relevance of curricula and to promoting the economic, cultural, and social development of the community, region, or country concerned, as well as the development of the individual and the groups involved; (e) contribute to improving employment and career prospects for every individual, regardless of gender, by offering equal opportunities to access all levels of formal and non-formal education and vocational and technological training in various sectors of urban and rural economies; (f) promote research concerning the planning and implementation of curricula, their assessment and development, as well as financing, administration, methods, materials, and teacher training; (g) promote understanding of the fundamental scientific principles applied across the diverse sectors of production. The activities in question should be framed within education for understanding, international cooperation and peace, and education relating to human rights and fundamental freedoms.

low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph emphasizes curriculum design (Design and development), implementation strategies (Curriculum implementation), evaluation and reform processes (Curriculum evaluation and reform), and competency-based outcomes (Competency-based and learning outcomes). It focuses on integrating work-related activities into education, aligning with structural and pedagogical aspects rather than subject domains or cross-cutting themes.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes respect for workers and the world of work (human rights and equality), balanced development (life skills), education for understanding and international cooperation (global citizenship), and equal opportunities (inclusive education). Categories like 'curriculum' and 'policy theme' are excluded as they are not explicitly mentioned in the paragraph.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph focuses on curriculum integration of work-related activities (Curriculum and pedagogy policies), mentions equal opportunities and gender (Inclusion and diversity), references international cooperation and peace (International cooperation and partnerships), and links to community development and environmental aspects (Sustainable development and environment).
low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes both academic development (general aims, moral/aesthetic values, scientific principles) and vocational elements (productive work, career prospects, TVET training). 'Combined general and vocational' captures the integration of work-related activities into education. 'General (academic)' reflects the broader educational objectives. 'Vocational/technical (TVET)' is excluded as the focus is on integration rather than standalone TVET.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph emphasizes curriculum integration, which involves teachers and educators in planning and implementing activities. Learners and students are directly impacted by these educational activities. Parents and communities are mentioned in relation to societal and community development. Civil society and NGOs are implied through references to international cooperation, human rights, and broader societal values.

TITLE: l'enseignement technique et professionnel, adoptée par
paragraph: 6. Provisions allowing children and young people to engage in productive work as part of their education should, in all cases, comply with national and international legislation and standards related to the employment of children and young people and, more specifically, with international conventions and recommendations concerning the minimum age for entering the workforce, as well as with national and international legislation and standards regarding the protection of children, young people, and other categories of individuals.

low_level_thinking.Legal frameworks: The paragraph explicitly references compliance with national and international legislation and standards, including international conventions and recommendations, directly linking to 'Education laws and regulations' and 'International conventions and treaties'. 'Policy implementation and enforcement' is less directly supported as the focus is on legislative compliance rather than enforcement mechanisms.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes compliance with national and international legislation, aligning with 'Governance and planning' for regulatory frameworks. 'International cooperation and partnerships' is relevant due to references to international conventions and recommendations. Other categories like 'Learner population' or 'Cross-cutting themes' were excluded as the focus is on legal compliance rather than direct learner protections or skills.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes compliance with international conventions protecting children and young people, aligning with 'Human rights and equality'. It also mentions protections for children and other vulnerable groups, fitting 'Inclusive education and special needs'. 'Other cross-cutting theme' was excluded as the paragraph's focus is explicitly on rights and inclusion, not a broader theme.

TITLE: l'enseignement technique et professionnel, adoptée par
paragraph: 7. Education and activities related to work should be equally accessible to persons of both sexes and should be chosen so as to combat prejudices in society in general and in the world of work in particular, while taking into account the relevant sociocultural specificities.

low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph mentions both education and work-related activities, indicating a dual focus on general and vocational aspects. 'Combined general and vocational' was excluded as the text does not explicitly frame them as integrated.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes equal access to education (human rights and equality) and combating prejudices through education (life skills). 'Inclusive education and special needs' is excluded as the focus is on gender equality, not specific disabilities. 'Other cross-cutting theme' is avoided due to the clear alignment with existing categories.

TITLE: l'enseignement technique et professionnel, adoptée par
paragraph: 8. The participation of school youth in productive activities should not involve any form of exploitation to their detriment.

low_level_thinking.Legal frameworks: The paragraph addresses the rights of school youth and the obligations of stakeholders to avoid exploitation, aligning with 'Rights and obligations of stakeholders'. It also implies policy enforcement to prevent exploitation, fitting 'Policy implementation and enforcement'. 'Constitution and fundamental rights' and 'International conventions and treaties' are less directly supported, while 'Legal disputes and interpretation' is not mentioned.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes protecting school youth from exploitation, aligning with health and well-being policies by safeguarding their welfare. Inclusion and diversity is relevant as it addresses the vulnerability of school youth as a specific population. Other categories like governance or legal frameworks are not directly mentioned.
low_level_thinking.Learner population: The paragraph explicitly mentions 'school youth' aligning with 'Youth and adolescents'. 'Low-income and disadvantaged groups' is inferred as 'exploitation to their detriment' implies vulnerability. Other categories like 'Children' are excluded as the focus is on school-age youth, not all children.

TITLE: l'enseignement technique et professionnel, adoptée par
paragraph: 9. Programs facilitating interaction between education and productive work should be developed with a view to lifelong education. Provisions aimed at promoting interactions between the education of children and young people and active life should ensure the continuity of these interactions and apply to all relevant disciplines.

low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes lifelong education and continuity between education and active life, which aligns with systemic integration of general and vocational aspects. 'Combined general and vocational' captures both academic (lifelong education) and TVET (productive work, active life) elements. 'General' and 'Vocational/technical' are excluded as the focus is on their integration rather than standalone emphasis.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph explicitly mentions 'lifelong education' and systemic provisions for continuity between education and active life, aligning with 'Lifelong learning and adult education'. 'Governance and planning' is supported by references to developing programs and ensuring continuity across disciplines. 'Curriculum' and 'Pedagogy' are excluded as the focus is on systemic integration, not specific educational content.
low_level_thinking.Learner population: The paragraph emphasizes lifelong education and continuity between education and active life, covering all age groups. 'All learners' captures the broad scope. 'Adults and lifelong learners' aligns with 'lifelong education'. 'Youth and adolescents' matches 'children and young people'. Categories like 'Children (0-17)' and 'Adults' are too narrow, while 'Indigenous' and 'Migrants' lack textual support.

TITLE: l'enseignement technique et professionnel, adoptée par
paragraph: 10. National experiences in the field of interaction between education and productive work should be encouraged through technical, financial, and equipment assistance to production units within education and training centers.

low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes technical assistance to production units within education and training centers, aligning with vocational/technical (TVET) focus. 'General (academic)' is excluded as the text prioritizes productive work integration over general academic education. 'Combined' is not selected due to lack of explicit mention of both general and vocational elements.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph mentions technical, financial, and equipment assistance, aligning with 'Financing and resource mobilization'. 'International cooperation and partnerships' is supported by the reference to national experiences being encouraged through collaborative support to production units. Other categories like 'Curriculum' or 'Infrastructure' are excluded as the focus is on resource support rather than educational content or physical infrastructure.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes technical, financial, and equipment assistance to production units within education and training centers, aligning with entrepreneurship and business skills. Financial and economic literacy is also relevant due to the mention of financial assistance. Other categories like 'Infrastructure & resources' are excluded as the focus is on educational integration, not physical infrastructure.

TITLE: l'enseignement technique et professionnel, adoptée par
paragraph: 11. Research and the production of teaching manuals should be promoted to facilitate the development of the links between education and productive work.

low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph emphasizes promoting research and teaching manual production to develop links between education and productive work, aligning with 'Design and development' for curriculum creation. 'Curriculum implementation' is relevant as it involves putting these developments into practice. 'Curriculum evaluation and reform' is excluded as the paragraph does not mention assessment or reform. 'Competency-based and learning outcomes' is not directly addressed, and other categories like 'Education orientation' are not explicitly covered.
low_level_thinking.Pedagogy & assessment: The paragraph emphasizes promoting research and teaching manuals to link education with productive work, aligning with pedagogical foundations and teaching methods. 'Assessment and feedback' is excluded as the text focuses on curriculum development over evaluation. 'Other pedagogy related issue' is not used due to specific mention of research and teaching materials.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes linking education to productive work, aligning with entrepreneurship and business skills. 'Life skills and 21st century competencies' is included as it relates to workforce readiness. Other categories like digital literacy or human rights are not explicitly mentioned, and 'inclusive education' is not directly relevant here.

TITLE: l'enseignement technique et professionnel, adoptée par
paragraph: 12. The authorities responsible for education should always, in agreement with social partners, participate in the supervision and control of the organization of general and technological training for youth and adults.

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph mentions supervision and control of training for youth and adults, aligning with lifelong learning and adult education. Governance and planning is supported by the reference to authorities and social partners in collaborative governance.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph mentions 'authorities responsible for education' which aligns with 'Government and policy makers'. 'Social partners' are explicitly referenced in the agreement, fitting the 'Stakeholder focus' high-level label. Other categories like 'Teachers and educators' or 'Parents and communities' are not directly mentioned.

TITLE: l'enseignement technique et professionnel, adoptée par
paragraph: 13. Educational activities involving interaction between education and active life should: (a) stimulate the development of creative skills and attitudes and help to understand the importance of management, scientific activity, and research in the workplace within modern society; as far as possible, pupils and especially students should be associated with more comprehensive practical activities, scientific research, study projects, medical, technical, and organizational assistance, as well as socio-cultural activities, in collaboration with teaching staff and other specialists, thereby contributing to national development; (b) familiarize pupils and students with the living and working conditions related to a wide range of economic activities corresponding to the current and future needs of the community or region and society as a whole; (c) expand the theoretical and practical knowledge and experience necessary for the correct and safe handling of tools, machines, and other equipment and materials that may be used in work; (d) help to better understand the economic and social relations established in active life with particular attention to the preservation of the physical environment and encourage the active participation of pupils and students in economic and social life; (e) emphasize the acquisition of socially useful theoretical and practical knowledge, the understanding of the interest and constraints of teamwork, and the development of problem-solving and management skills; (f) ensure the active participation of pupils and students in the organization of group work as well as in the planning of work processes and the evaluation of their results, by developing various forms of management; (g) encourage the transmission of national artisanal traditions to younger generations by organizing optional professional initiation courses.

low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes practical skills (tools, machines, equipment), real-world economic activities, and work-related knowledge, aligning with TVET. It also integrates theoretical understanding with societal engagement and teamwork, supporting 'Combined general and vocational'. 'General (academic)' is excluded as the focus is not on pure academic content. 'Learning modality' and 'Pedagogy & assessment' are not explicitly addressed. 'Policy theme' is omitted due to lack of explicit policy language.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes practical life skills (e.g., problem-solving, teamwork, management), aligning with 'Life skills and 21st century competencies'. 'Sustainable development and climate action' is supported by references to environmental preservation. 'Entrepreneurship and business skills' matches focus on economic activities and workplace management. Other categories like citizenship or digital literacy lack direct evidence.
low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph emphasizes designing educational activities that integrate practical skills (Design and development), focuses on competencies like problem-solving and teamwork (Competency-based and learning outcomes), and outlines implementation strategies involving collaboration and practical engagement (Curriculum implementation). 'Content and learning areas' is too narrow, 'Organization and instructional time' lacks direct mention, and 'Curriculum evaluation and reform' is not addressed.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph emphasizes collaboration between teaching staff and specialists (Teachers and educators), involves learners in practical activities (Learners and students), mentions partnerships with industry for skill development (Private sector and industry), and implies community engagement through socio-cultural activities (Parents and communities). Government and international organizations are not explicitly mentioned, while civil society is only indirectly referenced.

TITLE: l'enseignement technique et professionnel, adoptée par
paragraph: At the primary school level, they should primarily aim to familiarize children with the world of work in general, its various aspects and the broad categories of professions, as well as with the general social and material characteristics of different fields of economic activity, in particular ensuring that children become aware of the social value of all types of work and are introduced to group work, problem-solving, and research on issues affecting their own environment.

low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph specifies content related to work, professions, and social characteristics (Content and learning areas). It also mentions skills like group work, problem-solving, and research, aligning with competency-based outcomes. 'Curriculum implementation' and 'evaluation' are not explicitly addressed. 'Design and development' is too vague here. 'Education level' is mentioned but not a sub-label category.
low_level_thinking.Education level: The paragraph explicitly mentions 'primary school level' which directly maps to 'Primary education (ISCED 1)'. Other categories like 'Early childhood educational development (ISCED 01)' and 'Pre-primary education (ISCED 02)' are excluded as the text specifies primary school, not pre-primary or early childhood care. 'Multiple levels' is not selected as the focus is clearly on primary education.

General (academic) is excluded as the focus is on work-related skills rather than pure academic content.

TITLE: l'enseignement technique et professionnel, adoptée par
paragraph: 15. The curriculum at this level must include an introduction to creative activities, to the most common tools and machines, to materials, to working methods and production processes, and provide a preliminary understanding of working and production conditions, as well as the fundamental principles of science and technology as they apply to major economic sectors, along with the formative value of their products and services.

TITLE: l'enseignement technique et professionnel, adoptée par
paragraph: 16. Specific programs should provide for an initial study of the relationship between productive work and the areas to which children and adolescents attach value.

TITLE: l'enseignement technique et professionnel, adoptée par
paragraph: 17. At an appropriate level of the education system, the curricula could be more extensive and include the study of several types of activities and professions to prepare for the choice of an educational and vocational training pathway or a career. This more in-depth study of various economic sectors may include visits to workplaces, meetings with workers, and, where applicable, direct participation in production, alongside the study of fundamental principles concerning the economy of production, social and economic relations in professional activities, career prospects in various professions, as well as other issues that may be useful to pupils and students in selecting an educational and vocational training pathway, a job, or a profession.

low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph discusses expanding curricula to include vocational and economic content, supporting 'Design and development' for curriculum structure changes. 'Other curriculum-related issue' was excluded as the focus is specific to content design rather than broader administrative aspects.

It also integrates general economic principles and career guidance, supporting 'Combined general and vocational'. 'General (academic)' is excluded as the focus is on vocational pathways, not purely academic content.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes economic sectors, career prospects, and vocational training, aligning with financial/economic literacy. Entrepreneurship is implied through career exploration and production participation. Life skills are covered through decision-making and career pathway selection. Other categories like citizenship or health are not explicitly mentioned.

TITLE: l'enseignement technique et professionnel, adoptée par
paragraph: 18. Teaching programs may, where appropriate, include provisions for pre-vocational education and training aimed at acquiring the most suitable theoretical and practical knowledge with regard to the socio-economic environment or to professional fields to which the pupil or student wishes to gain access or may in fact have access later. They may also provide for direct participation in professional activities having particular educational value.

'General (academic)' is excluded as the focus is on vocational integration, not purely academic.

TITLE: l'enseignement technique et professionnel, adoptée par
paragraph: 19. Professional and technical education and training programmes should, as far as possible, be designed to prepare for occupations that are not narrowly specialised, so as to establish an interaction between general education, theoretical instruction, and practical training in workshops and other similar facilities, including fieldwork. For this purpose, the involvement of specialists is desirable.

low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph emphasizes designing programs to integrate general education with practical training (Design and development). It mentions content areas like theoretical instruction and fieldwork (Content and learning areas). The focus on preparing for non-specialized occupations implies competency-based outcomes (Competency-based and learning outcomes). 'Other curriculum-related issue' is excluded as the text directly addresses design, content, and outcomes rather than broader systemic issues.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes integrating general education with practical training and interaction between theory and practice, aligning with 'Life skills and 21st century competencies'. 'Other cross-cutting theme' is included due to the focus on cross-cutting themes bridging theoretical and practical aspects. low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph emphasizes designing education programs with input from specialists, aligning with 'Teachers and educators' as they design curricula. 'Private sector and industry' is implied through references to occupations and practical training in workshops, which often involve industry collaboration. Other categories like government or civil society are not explicitly mentioned.

TITLE: l'enseignement technique et professionnel, adoptée par
paragraph: 20. Apprentices and other individuals undergoing job training who are primarily or jointly under the responsibility of authorities outside the education sector or employers should necessarily have sufficient opportunities to attend school part-time or to take courses related to their training or of a more general nature. This can be facilitated through a system of staggered or grouped leave, alternating courses, or other similar arrangements. The conditions of apprentices should be improved, both regarding their rights and obligations as workers and their right to comprehensive education. Training supervisors should duly consider the educational needs of those entrusted to their care, including the necessity of ensuring their future mobility. Education authorities and educational institutions should take measures to gradually introduce these opportunities or participate in the development and ensure the implementation of such measures.

low_level_thinking.Learning modality: The paragraph emphasizes part-time schooling and training opportunities for apprentices, aligning with 'Non-formal education' as it involves structured but non-traditional learning arrangements. 'Lifelong learning' is supported by the focus on continuous education and skill development throughout an individual's career. 'Formal education' is excluded as the context involves non-traditional learning settings, and 'Informal learning' is less fitting due to the structured nature of the proposed measures.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes part-time schooling and training opportunities for apprentices, aligning with 'Lifelong learning and adult education'. It also addresses access to education for specific groups, supporting 'Access and equity'. 'Governance and planning' is relevant due to the mention of systemic improvements and institutional responsibilities. 'Quality and learning outcomes' and 'Curriculum and pedagogy policies' are less directly supported, while 'Other policy theme' is avoided due to specificity.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph emphasizes government and education authorities' responsibility to implement policies (Government and policy makers). It mentions employers and training supervisors (Private sector and industry) and apprentices/trainees (Learners and students). TITLE: l'enseignement technique et professionnel, adoptée par

TITLE: l'enseignement technique et professionnel, adoptée par
paragraph: 23. In adult education, the interactions between productive work and education must be established: (a) by planning educational activities as an integral part of national plans and programs for technical and economic development, the reform of professional structures, the fight against underemployment and unemployment, and the development of new economic activities; (b) by offering educational opportunities that meet the needs of countries on the one hand, and of individuals and groups striving for better jobs or improved advancement prospects on the other; (c) by appropriately applying the principles of functional teaching of reading and arithmetic, as well as creativity and critical thinking.

paragraph: 28. TITLE: l'enseignement technique et professionnel, adoptée par
paragraph: 33. Measures aimed at improving the interactions between education and productive work, so as to involve the world of work both inside and outside the school, should be carefully planned and programmed. This planning and programming should, where appropriate, include the following steps: (a) determination of educational objectives at all levels; (b) consultation with the competent government authorities, educational organizations, employers’ and workers’ organizations, parents’ associations, teachers and students, and other relevant bodies to ensure that the pre-vocational and vocational educational programs provided by the school, on the one hand, and the initial training programs offered by industrial and agricultural enterprises, on the other hand, complement each other and are well designed; (c) informing the general public, parents, competent authorities, and other relevant bodies about the objectives pursued and the main orientations of the programs and their implementation; (d) planning and research, including, where appropriate, pilot programs and continuous evaluation of the cost, performance, and effectiveness of these programs; (e) identification of links between disciplines and, where appropriate, modification of curricula; (f) plans for the generalization of the program to the entire targeted population, including, as appropriate, budget allocations, agreements with employers and workers, arrangements for the reception and placement of trainees or students, training and information for all those within and outside the participating educational institutions, and training of teaching staff, including teachers of related subjects; (g) modification, where appropriate, of administrative provisions applicable to the actions of the educational authorities and institutions concerned, in order to facilitate exchanges and permanent dialogue between these authorities, other competent educational bodies, and the world of work; (h) agreements with representatives of the world of work so that they, on their part, make corresponding arrangements; (i) provisions for the programming and support of research activities aimed at improving program design, the selection and design of creative work and other practical activities within educational institutions or corresponding arrangements outside, the definition of standards for such arrangements, evaluation of programs and projects, and other relevant purposes; (j) involvement of pupils and students in the programming and execution of productive work as well as in the evaluation of its results through the application of various forms of participation and management; (k) promotion of the introduction, within the field of educational sciences and higher education centers, of courses linking education and productive work; (l) promotion, within technical training centers, of various types of courses related to production techniques.

low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes integration of education with productive work, including vocational training programs and curriculum modifications (TVET). 'Combined general and vocational' applies due to mentions of both academic and practical components. 'General (academic)' is excluded as the focus is on work integration, not purely academic.
low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph emphasizes planning and programming (Design and development), implementation steps like stakeholder consultation and program generalization (Curriculum implementation), and evaluation through continuous assessment and research (Curriculum evaluation and reform). Categories like 'Content and learning areas' and 'Competency-based and learning outcomes' are less directly supported, while 'Stakeholder focus' is covered under 'Curriculum implementation'.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph emphasizes collaboration with multiple stakeholders including government authorities, teachers, students, parents, and the private sector. These groups are explicitly mentioned as key participants in planning and implementation, supporting the 'Stakeholder focus' high-level label. Categories like civil society and international organizations are not directly referenced, so they are excluded.

Other categories like 'education system monitoring & evaluation' are implied but not explicitly stated, while 'policy theme' is the high-level label.

'Other cross-cutting theme' captures broader systemic planning and policy aspects not fully covered by other categories.

TITLE: l'enseignement technique et professionnel, adoptée par
paragraph: 38. Member States should cooperate at the subregional, regional, and international levels in the development of programs and practices aimed at improving the interactions between education and the world of work within their territories. This cooperation should take the form of: (a) a systematic and ongoing exchange of information and experiences through, in particular, seminars, meetings, and conferences organized at the national, subregional, regional, or international level, focusing on the principles and practices related to the design and implementation of programs intended to enhance the connections between education, productive work, and the world of work in general; (b) joint experiences in the implementation of these programs and the evaluation of their performance, effectiveness, and impact; (c) the exchange of administrators and teachers involved in programs promoting interaction between education, productive work, and the world of work in general; (d) subregional, regional, and international cooperation in the field of educational research; (e) the promotion of regional meetings where national experiences in this area may be compared.

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes international cooperation through subregional, regional, and international collaboration (directly matching 'International cooperation and partnerships'). 'Other policy theme' was excluded as the text specifies concrete cooperation forms rather than vague themes.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph emphasizes international cooperation (international organizations and donors), joint program implementation (government and policy makers), and connections to the world of work (private sector and industry). TITLE: l'enseignement technique et professionnel, adoptée par
paragraph: 39. Within the framework of its Regular Programme and its network of institutions for educational innovation for development—the Asian Programme of Educational Innovation for Development (APEID), the Network of Educational Innovation for Development in Africa (NEIDA), the Programme for Cooperation in Educational Innovation Research and Development in South-East Europe (CODIESEE), the Educational Innovation Programme for Development in Arab States (EIPDAS), the Network of Educational Innovation for Development in the Caribbean (CARNEID)—and in collaboration with other international and regional institutions and organizations, particularly the International Labour Organization, the Food and Agriculture Organization, the United Nations Development Programme, and UNICEF, UNESCO should take the necessary measures to ensure (a) an organized exchange of information and documentation on measures taken in various countries and regions to improve interactions between education and work, including the provision of detailed information on the design, methodology, implementation modalities, and evaluation of projects related to the interaction between education and productive work, making use of opportunities offered by computers and other modern information technologies; (b) technical cooperation services, provided upon request, to facilitate the planning, organization, and implementation of programs developed for this purpose, as well as the evaluation of their performance, effectiveness, and impact; (c) the organization at the international or regional level of conferences, meetings, and seminars on issues and problems related to the creation and development of interactions between education and working life in general or regarding specific groups of people and different types and levels of education.

TITLE: L'AMELIORATION DE L'ENSEIGNEMENT SECONDAIRE : OBJECTIFS, STRUCTURES, CONTENUS ET METHODES PREAMBULE
paragraph: 1. Secondary education is a phase of education during which young people are gradually led to become autonomous and responsible adults. During this period, young people acquire the knowledge and skills necessary for their preparation for life in all its aspects.

low_level_thinking.Education level: The paragraph explicitly mentions 'secondary education' and describes it as a phase where young people acquire knowledge and skills for life. Other categories like primary or tertiary are excluded as the text does not mention those levels.
