(c) ensuring high-level teaching, training, and research, which should be regarded as a primary task amid the rapid expansion of postsecondary enrollment; (d) guaranteeing equal opportunities for all those with the required capacities to access this level of education, in preparation for life and, more specifically, for professional life, as well as in participation in the various activities of society; (e) promoting the flourishing and harmonious development of the individual by providing the necessary conditions for intellectual, moral, professional, spiritual, physical, aesthetic, and social formation; (f) aiming for better quantitative and qualitative alignment of the education provided with the continually evolving demands of the employment situation; (g) involving various social and economic partners more closely in the effective functioning and development of this level of education; (h) increasing the contribution of postsecondary education, especially universities, to the strengthening and expansion of lifelong education; (i) enhancing the contribution of postsecondary education to the development of other levels and forms of education within the framework of lifelong learning, particularly its contribution to training and research efforts aimed at the substantial reduction of illiteracy; (j) encouraging universities and other higher education institutions to also function as centers for individual and distance learning within the perspective of lifelong education; (k) promoting education for understanding, cooperation, and international peace, as well as education relating to human rights and fundamental freedoms; (l) contributing to highlighting the culture and cultural identity of each country; (m) contributing to the solution of global problems faced by society and to international intellectual cooperation; (n) promoting the mobility of teachers and students at the international level.

low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes both academic principles (e.g., high-level teaching, research, cultural identity) and vocational aspects (e.g., alignment with employment demands, lifelong education, training for illiteracy reduction). 'Combined general and vocational' captures the integration of academic and practical elements, while 'General (academic)' reflects the focus on intellectual and moral development. 'Vocational/technical (TVET)' is excluded as the emphasis is broader than technical training alone.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes quality through high-level teaching and alignment with employment needs (Quality and learning outcomes). Lifelong learning is directly mentioned with references to lifelong education and adult training (Lifelong learning and adult education). International cooperation is highlighted through global issues, international peace, and student mobility (International cooperation and partnerships). Inclusion and diversity are addressed via equal opportunities and cultural identity (Inclusion and diversity). Sustainable development is tied to scientific foundations and global challenges (Sustainable development and environment).
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes global issues (global citizenship), human rights (fundamental freedoms), sustainable development (socio-economic and technological development), and equal opportunities (inclusive education). 'Other cross-cutting theme' was excluded as specific categories better fit the content.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph emphasizes policy guidance (government/policy makers), teaching/research roles (teachers/educators), learner access and development (learners/students), partnerships with industry (private sector), and international cooperation (international organizations). Social partners are mentioned but not explicitly listed as a category, so 'private sector' and 'international organizations' are prioritized. 'Civil society' and 'parents' are not directly referenced.
low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph outlines guiding principles for curriculum design (Design and development) and specifies learning areas like cultural identity and global issues (Content and learning areas). Competency-based outcomes are implied through references to skills for professional life and international cooperation. 'Other curriculum-related issue' covers lifelong education and mobility aspects not fitting other categories.

TITLE: LA DIVERSIFICATION DE L'ENSEIGNEMENT POSTSECONDAIRE FACE A LA SITUATION DE L'KMPLOI Préambule
paragraph: 4. It would be appropriate to regularly review the objectives and functions of postsecondary education to ensure that they meet new social, economic, and cultural needs as well as technological developments. Given the significant changes in the composition of the student population, and to remain aligned with advances in science and technology and the resulting transformation of the labor market, postsecondary educational institutions should adopt a more flexible and adaptable structure, particularly regarding admission criteria, the duration of studies, curricula, teaching methods, and standards and methods of practical training.

low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph emphasizes reviewing and adapting curricula (Curriculum evaluation and reform), implementing flexible structures (Curriculum implementation), and adjusting instructional time and organization (Organization and instructional time). 'Content and learning areas' is excluded as the focus is on structure, not content. 'Competency-based and learning outcomes' is not explicitly mentioned. 'Design and development' is too broad and not directly referenced.
low_level_thinking.Pedagogy & assessment: The paragraph emphasizes adapting teaching methods and approaches (e.g., flexible structures, curricula, and practical training) and mentions technological developments, supporting 'Technology-supported pedagogy'. 'Pedagogical foundations and theories' is excluded as the text focuses on implementation rather than theoretical frameworks.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes adapting curricula, teaching methods, and structures (curriculum/pedagogy). Governance is addressed through structural flexibility and alignment with labor market changes. Innovation/tech policies are implied by references to technological developments and labor market transformation.
low_level_thinking.Education system monitoring & evaluation: The paragraph emphasizes adapting postsecondary education structures and methods to meet societal and technological changes, aligning with 'Quality assurance and accreditation' for standards and flexibility. 'Institutional evaluation and inspection' fits as it mentions reviewing objectives and functions. 'Education system monitoring & evaluation' is the high-level label, so sub-labels focus on structural and methodological adaptations, not broader monitoring.
low_level_thinking.Learner population: The paragraph mentions 'significant changes in the composition of the student population' and calls for flexible structures, which aligns with 'Adults and lifelong learners' needing adaptable education. 'Low-income and disadvantaged groups' are implied by the need for structural changes to meet economic and social needs. Other categories like 'Youth and adolescents' or 'Indigenous or minority groups' are not explicitly referenced.

TITLE: LA DIVERSIFICATION DE L'ENSEIGNEMENT POSTSECONDAIRE FACE A LA SITUATION DE L'KMPLOI Préambule
paragraph: 5. The policies intended to govern postsecondary education in the 1990s should take into account the anticipated developments up to the year 2000 and beyond, which will likely result in radical changes in the size and composition of the workforce, with numerous implications for postsecondary education. Adequate solutions should also be developed to address unemployment arising from the application of technology and the advanced automation of production and services, by broadly expanding access to lifelong education and retraining courses.

low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph explicitly mentions technology, automation, and retraining, directly linking to 'Digital and ICT literacy' and 'Life skills and 21st century competencies'. These categories capture the focus on adapting to technological changes and lifelong learning. Other categories like 'Entrepreneurship' or 'Human rights' are not explicitly addressed in the text.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph explicitly mentions lifelong education and retraining courses, aligning with 'Lifelong learning and adult education'. Technology and automation are directly linked to 'Innovation and technology policies'. Workforce changes and unemployment due to technology tie to 'Teachers and workforce policies'. Other categories like access or curriculum are not explicitly addressed.
low_level_thinking.Learner population: The paragraph emphasizes lifelong education and retraining in response to technological changes, aligning with 'Adults and lifelong learners'. Mention of addressing unemployment and workforce changes suggests targeting disadvantaged groups. Other categories like 'Children' or 'Youth' are not explicitly mentioned, and no specific reference to disabilities, minorities, or migrants is present.
low_level_thinking.Learning modality: The paragraph explicitly mentions 'lifelong education and retraining courses' which directly maps to 'Lifelong learning'. 'Non-formal education' is supported by the context of addressing unemployment through expanded access to training programs, which typically fall under non-formal education. 'Formal education' and 'Informal learning' are excluded as the text focuses on structured retraining (non-formal) rather than traditional formal schooling or unstructured informal learning.
low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph emphasizes expanding access to lifelong education and retraining courses to address unemployment from technology/automation, aligning with 'Curriculum implementation' (deployment of educational programs). 'Competency-based and learning outcomes' fits as the focus is on skills development and outcomes (e.g., addressing workforce changes through retraining). 'Curriculum evaluation and reform' is excluded as there's no mention of assessing or revising curricula, and 'Other' is avoided due to specificity.
low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes retraining and lifelong education in response to technological changes, aligning with Vocational/technical (TVET). 'Combined general and vocational' is included as the solution involves both workforce adaptation (vocational) and broader education access (general). 'General (academic)' is excluded since the focus is on practical skills and workforce needs, not academic general education.

TITLE: LA DIVERSIFICATION DE L'ENSEIGNEMENT POSTSECONDAIRE FACE A LA SITUATION DE L'KMPLOI Préambule
paragraph: 6. In determining the resources to be allocated to postsecondary education and the distribution of these resources among the various sectors, disciplines, and institutions, full attention must be given to long-term objectives, so that this education can adapt to evolving needs and fulfill its fundamental role within society.

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes long-term objectives and adaptability in resource allocation, aligning with 'Governance and planning' for strategic decision-making. 'Quality and learning outcomes' is relevant as it addresses fulfilling education's societal role through adaptive quality. Other categories like 'Curriculum' or 'Financing' are not explicitly mentioned, and 'Innovation' is not directly referenced.
low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes long-term objectives and societal role of education, aligning with general academic orientation. Vocational/technical (TVET) is excluded as the focus is on adaptability and societal role rather than specific skills training. Combined general and vocational is not selected as there's no explicit mention of integrating both.

TITLE: LA DIVERSIFICATION DE L'ENSEIGNEMENT POSTSECONDAIRE FACE A LA SITUATION DE L'KMPLOI Préambule
paragraph: At this level of education, it is essential not only to promote a more equitable distribution of educational resources between youth and adult workers but also to ensure the extension of access to these resources to remote areas as well as to marginalized, minority, and disadvantaged groups and to persons with disabilities, particularly through local development programs and organizations. Postsecondary education institutions should take the necessary measures to accommodate the increasing numbers of non-traditional students, such as part-time adult students, students from modest socio-economic backgrounds, and workers requiring stimulation through courses for updating their knowledge, skill development, or retraining. Furthermore, it is necessary to respect the right of populations in affected territories to preserve their national and cultural identity.

low_level_thinking.Education level: The paragraph mentions postsecondary education institutions and non-traditional students, aligning with ISCED 4. It also references higher education through skill development and retraining, suggesting ISCED 5-8. 'Multiple levels' is included due to the mention of both secondary and tertiary education contexts.
low_level_thinking.Learner population: The paragraph explicitly mentions adult workers, non-traditional students (including part-time adults), and persons with disabilities, supporting 'Adults and lifelong learners' and 'Learners with disabilities'. 'Indigenous or minority groups' aligns with references to marginalized, minority, and disadvantaged groups. 'Rural and remote learners' is directly mentioned through remote areas. 'Low-income and disadvantaged groups' is supported by references to socio-economic backgrounds and marginalized groups. 'Population in conflict and emergencies settings' is excluded as there is no mention of conflict or emergencies.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes equitable resource distribution, access for marginalized groups, and disability inclusion, aligning with 'Human rights and equality' and 'Inclusive education and special needs'. 'Other cross-cutting theme' captures cultural identity preservation mentioned, as no exact match exists. 'Citizenship' and 'Global citizenship' are excluded due to lack of direct mention of civic or peace education. 'Policy theme' is omitted as the focus is on rights and implementation, not legal frameworks.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes equitable resource distribution and access for marginalized groups, aligning with 'Access and equity'. It discusses non-traditional students and adult learners, fitting 'Lifelong learning and adult education'. Cultural identity preservation and inclusion of disabled persons support 'Inclusion and diversity'. 'Policy theme' is implied but not explicitly labeled, while other categories like 'Governance' or 'Financing' lack direct evidence.
low_level_thinking.Legal frameworks: The paragraph emphasizes legal obligations (e.g., 'ensure the extension of access', 'respect the right of populations') aligning with 'Rights and obligations of stakeholders'. 'Education laws and regulations' fits as it discusses institutional measures and access policies. 'Policy implementation and enforcement' is supported by references to 'take the necessary measures' and 'local development programs'. 'Constitution and fundamental rights' is excluded as the focus is on educational policy rather than constitutional provisions. 'International conventions and treaties' is not mentioned. 'Legal disputes and interpretation' lacks evidence. 'Other' is avoided due to specificity.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph emphasizes non-traditional learners (students from modest backgrounds, part-time adults) and marginalized groups, supporting 'Learners and students'. Local development programs and organizations align with 'Civil society and NGOs'. 'Other' covers cultural identity and legal rights themes not fully captured by other categories.

TITLE: LA DIVERSIFICATION DE L'ENSEIGNEMENT POSTSECONDAIRE FACE A LA SITUATION DE L'KMPLOI Préambule
paragraph: 8. With regard to the expansion of postsecondary education, priority should be given, both in teaching and research, to disciplines experiencing rapid development due to advances in science and technology, as well as to meeting labor market demands and other essential societal needs.

low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph prioritizes science/tech disciplines (general academic) and labor market demands (vocational/technical), supporting combined focus. 'Combined general and vocational' was excluded as the paragraph does not explicitly merge these into a single integrated framework.
low_level_thinking.Subject domain: The paragraph explicitly mentions 'disciplines experiencing rapid development due to advances in science and technology' directly linking to STEM. 'Vocational and technical skills' aligns with 'meeting labor market demands'. 'Subject domain' is explicitly mentioned as the high-level label. 'Policy theme' and 'Curriculum' were excluded as the focus is on subject prioritization, not policy or curriculum structure.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes prioritizing science/tech disciplines (Innovation and technology policies) and aligning curricula with labor market needs (Curriculum and pedagogy policies). Governance and planning is included as it involves strategic prioritization of disciplines. 'Policy theme' was excluded as the focus is on specific policy areas rather than overarching themes.

TITLE: LA DIVERSIFICATION DE L'ENSEIGNEMENT POSTSECONDAIRE FACE A LA SITUATION DE L'KMPLOI Préambule
paragraph: 9. Within the framework of national priorities, efforts in planning as well as coordination and harmonization by public authorities should ensure an orderly development of postsecondary education. It would be appropriate to improve the coherence of the entire postsecondary system, particularly regarding universities and their relationships with other institutions focused on professional training, in order to ensure better complementarity between the different types of postsecondary education. To this end, it may be advisable to consider the establishment of permanent coordination mechanisms.

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes coordination, coherence, and systemic harmonization of postsecondary education, aligning with 'Governance and planning'. It also mentions improving complementarity between education types, which relates to 'Quality and learning outcomes'. 'Other policy theme' was excluded as the text directly addresses governance structures and systemic quality improvements.
low_level_thinking.Education system monitoring & evaluation: The paragraph emphasizes coordination mechanisms and coherence in postsecondary education systems, aligning with quality assurance (ensuring complementarity and systemic coherence) and institutional evaluation (establishing permanent coordination mechanisms). Learning assessments and EMIS/data systems are not explicitly mentioned, while research/surveys are not the focus. Education statistics are not directly referenced.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph focuses on coordination, coherence, and systemic harmonization in postsecondary education, which aligns with broader educational coordination themes. None of the specific categories (e.g., citizenship, digital literacy, sustainability) are explicitly mentioned. The 'Other cross-cutting theme' is the most appropriate as it captures systemic coordination without fitting specific narrower categories.

TITLE: LA DIVERSIFICATION DE L'ENSEIGNEMENT POSTSECONDAIRE FACE A LA SITUATION DE L'KMPLOI Préambule
paragraph: 10. Better articulation and continuity between secondary education and postsecondary education should be sought. Measures should also be taken to increase the contribution of postsecondary education to other levels of education, particularly regarding staff training and the development of educational research.

low_level_thinking.Education level: The paragraph explicitly mentions continuity between secondary and postsecondary education, covering ISCED 2/3 and ISCED 4. 'Multiple levels' is included due to the emphasis on articulation across education levels. Pre-primary, primary, and tertiary levels are not mentioned, so they are excluded.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes structural integration between education levels, aligning with 'Governance and planning'. It also mentions curriculum development and staff training, supporting 'Curriculum and pedagogy policies'. Other categories like 'Quality and learning outcomes' or 'Teachers and workforce policies' are not explicitly addressed.
low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph emphasizes improving articulation between education levels (Design and development) and implementing measures for postsecondary education's role (Curriculum implementation). 'Content and learning areas' is excluded as the focus is on structural integration, not specific content. 'Curriculum evaluation and reform' is not directly mentioned, though reform is implied, but the text prioritizes implementation over evaluation.

TITLE: LA DIVERSIFICATION DE L'ENSEIGNEMENT POSTSECONDAIRE FACE A LA SITUATION DE L'KMPLOI Préambule
paragraph: The development of policies and strategies, as well as the planning of postsecondary education development, should involve a broad process of research, evaluation, and consultation, involving not only the authorities responsible for this education but also representatives from various sectors of socio-economic life.

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes multi-sectoral consultation and stakeholder involvement, aligning with 'Governance and planning' through its focus on policy development processes. 'Inclusion and diversity' is supported by the mention of involving representatives from various socio-economic sectors, promoting broad participation. Other categories like 'Education system monitoring & evaluation' are excluded as the primary focus is on stakeholder engagement rather than evaluation mechanisms.

TITLE: LA DIVERSIFICATION DE L'ENSEIGNEMENT POSTSECONDAIRE FACE A LA SITUATION DE L'KMPLOI Préambule
paragraph: 12. The planning of postsecondary education should, whenever possible, take into account forecasts concerning future labor market needs. It would be desirable to improve the quality of forecasts of the demand for highly skilled labor; this information should be regularly made available to the authorities responsible for postsecondary education.

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph focuses on planning postsecondary education based on labor market forecasts, aligning with 'Governance and planning' through policy-driven decision-making. 'Quality and learning outcomes' is relevant as improving forecast quality enhances educational relevance. Other categories like 'Education system monitoring & evaluation' are excluded as the text lacks explicit evaluation mechanisms.
low_level_thinking.Education system monitoring & evaluation: The paragraph emphasizes labor market data integration and forecast quality, aligning with 'Education statistics and indicators' through data collection and reporting. 'Quality assurance and accreditation' fits as improving forecast quality is a form of quality assurance. Other categories like EMIS or institutional evaluation are not directly mentioned, and the focus on policy planning doesn't match 'learning assessments' or 'research'.

TITLE: LA DIVERSIFICATION DE L'ENSEIGNEMENT POSTSECONDAIRE FACE A LA SITUATION DE L'KMPLOI Préambule
paragraph: 13. It would be desirable to establish multidimensional criteria for admission to postsecondary educational institutions. These criteria should aim to ensure fairness, objectivity, and the consideration of all factors, as well as a balance between the interests of individuals and the needs of society.

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph discusses admission criteria ensuring fairness and balancing individual and societal interests, aligning with 'Access and equity'. It also mentions establishing multidimensional criteria, which relates to institutional governance and planning. 'Quality and learning outcomes' and 'Inclusion and diversity' were considered but focus on different aspects not explicitly addressed.
low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph focuses on admission criteria for postsecondary institutions, which aligns with general academic policies. Vocational/technical (TVET) is excluded as the text does not mention vocational training. Combined general and vocational is rejected since the paragraph does not integrate both streams.

TITLE: LA DIVERSIFICATION DE L'ENSEIGNEMENT POSTSECONDAIRE FACE A LA SITUATION DE L'KMPLOI Préambule
paragraph: 14. It is important that the skills required by the employment sector, particularly due to the use of computers and information technologies—such as versatility and adaptability, a broad general and technological culture, the ability to work in a group, communicate, and cooperate, a sense of responsibility and initiative, a taste for autonomous learning, etc.—be developed among future specialists.

low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph explicitly mentions 'use of computers and information technologies' directly supporting 'Digital and ICT literacy'. 'Versatility, adaptability, teamwork, communication, responsibility, initiative, autonomous learning' align with 'Life skills and 21st century competencies'. Other categories like 'Global citizenship' or 'Human rights' are not explicitly mentioned. 'Curriculum' is excluded as the focus is on competencies rather than program structure.
low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph emphasizes developing specific competencies (adaptability, technological literacy, collaboration) which aligns with 'Competency-based and learning outcomes'. The mention of integrating these skills into future specialists' education suggests a curriculum-related integration, justifying 'Other curriculum-related issue'. 'Content and learning areas' is too narrow, and 'Curriculum implementation' lacks direct mention of structural integration.

TITLE: LA DIVERSIFICATION DE L'ENSEIGNEMENT POSTSECONDAIRE FACE A LA SITUATION DE L'KMPLOI Préambule
paragraph: 15. Programs, textbooks, and teaching and learning methods should be regularly evaluated, revised, and adjusted, and new forms of university pedagogy should be explored to ensure they are adapted to changes in industrial, agricultural, and social processes, keep pace with the accelerated progress of scientific and technological advancement, and the resulting evolution of knowledge and skills. To this end, among other measures, an optimal balance should be sought for each field of study between general theoretical and practical instruction and training, practical internships in companies should be guaranteed for students, as well as an alternation between study and work periods. Interdisciplinary and multidisciplinary programs should be encouraged. It is desirable that theoretical and practical training be combined, as much as possible, with students’ initiation into research work, and that new technological options be used to enable students to acquire knowledge in a self-directed manner.

low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph emphasizes regular evaluation and revision of curricula (Curriculum evaluation and reform), structured alternation between study and work (Organization and instructional time), and self-directed learning through technology (Competency-based and learning outcomes). Interdisciplinary programs and research integration are excluded as they align more with 'Cross-cutting themes & skills' contested in the summary.
low_level_thinking.Pedagogy & assessment: The paragraph emphasizes teaching methods (alternating study/work, internships, interdisciplinary programs) and pedagogical foundations (adapting to societal changes, balancing theory/practice). Technology-supported pedagogy is explicitly mentioned with 'new technological options' for self-directed learning. 'Assessment and feedback' is excluded as evaluation is framed as curriculum revision, not assessment. 'Professional learning communities' lacks evidence. 'Cross-cutting themes' is not a sub-label here.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes self-directed learning (Digital and ICT literacy), 21st-century skills like adaptability and interdisciplinary approaches (Life skills and 21st century competencies), and broader systemic reforms (Other cross-cutting theme). Citizenship and civic education, human rights, and sustainability are not explicitly mentioned. Entrepreneurship and business skills are not directly addressed.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph focuses on curriculum evaluation, teaching methods, and pedagogical reforms (Curriculum and pedagogy policies). It mentions technology use for self-directed learning (Innovation and technology policies). Interdisciplinary programs and adapting to societal changes imply inclusion of diverse perspectives (Inclusion and diversity). Excluded categories like 'Governance' or 'Financing' are not addressed.

TITLE: LA DIVERSIFICATION DE L'ENSEIGNEMENT POSTSECONDAIRE FACE A LA SITUATION DE L'KMPLOI Préambule
paragraph: 16. It is important that postsecondary educational institutions now organize their teaching in connection with professional environments. To this end, representatives from economic and social sectors could be integrated as external experts on institutional boards, on expert committees responsible for developing study plans, and, where appropriate, on final evaluation panels.

low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph discusses integrating external experts into institutional boards and evaluation panels, aligning with 'Curriculum implementation' through collaboration. 'Curriculum evaluation and reform' is supported by mention of final evaluation panels and development of study plans. 'Design and development' is less precise, and other categories lack direct textual support.
low_level_thinking.Pedagogy & assessment: The paragraph mentions integrating external experts into institutional boards and evaluation panels, aligning with 'Assessment and feedback' and 'Professional learning communities'. 'Teaching methods and approaches' is supported by organizing teaching in connection with professional environments. 'Curriculum' and 'Education orientation' are excluded as the focus is on implementation and evaluation rather than curriculum design or broader orientation.

TITLE: LA DIVERSIFICATION DE L'ENSEIGNEMENT POSTSECONDAIRE FACE A LA SITUATION DE L'KMPLOI Préambule
paragraph: It is desirable that postsecondary educational institutions engage specialists from the labor market and research establishments as instructors and that appropriate legislative measures be taken to encourage such engagement.

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph mentions engaging specialists from labor market and research establishments as instructors, aligning with 'Teachers and workforce policies'. Legislative measures to encourage engagement relate to governance and planning frameworks. 'Other policy theme' was excluded as the focus is on specific policy areas, not a broad theme.
low_level_thinking.Teachers: The paragraph mentions engaging specialists from the labor market and research establishments as instructors, which relates to recruitment and hiring. It also refers to legislative measures, aligning with legal status and rights. Other categories like professional development or working conditions are not directly addressed.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph mentions 'specialists from the labor market and research establishments as instructors', which aligns with 'Teachers and educators' and 'Private sector and industry' (labor market). 'Research and academic institutions' is implied through 'research establishments', though not explicitly listed, it is the closest match. 'Government and policy makers' is excluded as legislative measures are mentioned but not as a focus. 'Other' is avoided due to specificity.

TITLE: LA DIVERSIFICATION DE L'ENSEIGNEMENT POSTSECONDAIRE FACE A LA SITUATION DE L'KMPLOI Préambule
paragraph: 18. At the same time, while ensuring that they enjoy the best conditions and appropriate means for the fulfillment of their mission, opportunities should be offered to full-time teachers and trainers in postsecondary educational institutions, particularly the younger ones, to undergo retraining, to supplement their knowledge, and to stay abreast of advances in science and technology under the actual conditions of the production process. To this end, the organization of internships in enterprises and research institutions intended for teachers and trainers should be encouraged.

low_level_thinking.Teachers: The paragraph emphasizes retraining, knowledge supplementation, and staying updated in science/technology, aligning with 'Professional development'. It also mentions 'best conditions and appropriate means' which relates to 'Working conditions and salaries'. Categories like 'Initial education and training' and 'Recruitment and hiring' are excluded as the focus is on ongoing development, not initial training or hiring processes.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph focuses on supporting teachers and trainers through retraining and internships, directly aligning with 'Teachers and educators'. Internships in enterprises and research institutions link to 'Private sector and industry'. Other categories like learners or parents are not mentioned. 'Infrastructure & resources' and 'Pedagogy & assessment' are not explicitly addressed.

TITLE: LA DIVERSIFICATION DE L'ENSEIGNEMENT POSTSECONDAIRE FACE A LA SITUATION DE L'KMPLOI Préambule
paragraph: 19. In general, when developing policies and strategies for postsecondary education, and particularly when establishing new fields or programs in this area, the training and professional development of teachers, educators, and other categories of educational staff should be given serious consideration. The creation of new institutions, fields, or programs should be accompanied by adequate support from qualified teaching and auxiliary personnel.

low_level_thinking.Teachers: The paragraph emphasizes professional development through 'training and professional development of teachers' and 'adequate support from qualified personnel', aligning with 'Professional development' and 'Initial education and training'. 'Teacher supply and shortages' is included due to the mention of 'qualified teaching and auxiliary personnel' implying workforce adequacy. 'Recruitment and hiring' is excluded as the text focuses on training/support rather than hiring processes. 'Working conditions and salaries' and 'Legal status and rights' are not explicitly addressed. 'Teacher evaluation and performance' is not mentioned. 'Qualification and certification' is indirectly related but not explicitly stated.
low_level_thinking.Infrastructure & resources: The paragraph emphasizes teacher training and professional development, aligning with 'Teaching and learning materials' through its focus on educational staff preparation. 'ICT and digital infrastructure' is implied by the mention of 'new fields or programs' requiring support, though not explicitly stated. Other categories like physical infrastructure or WASH are not directly mentioned.
low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph emphasizes designing policies and strategies for postsecondary education, aligning with 'Design and development'. It also discusses establishing new fields/programs and supporting them with personnel, fitting 'Curriculum implementation'. 'Content and learning areas' is excluded as the focus is on systemic support, not specific content. 'Curriculum evaluation and reform' is not mentioned. 'Competency-based' and 'Other' are irrelevant here.
low_level_thinking.Pedagogy & assessment: The paragraph emphasizes the importance of teacher training and professional development, aligning with 'Pedagogical foundations and theories' as it addresses the theoretical underpinnings of education. 'Professional learning communities' is also relevant due to the focus on ongoing support and development of educational staff. Categories like 'Teaching methods and approaches' and 'Assessment and feedback' are not explicitly mentioned, while 'Technology-supported pedagogy' lacks direct evidence.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph emphasizes teacher training and professional development, directly aligning with 'Teachers and educators'. It also mentions policy development and institutional support, linking to 'Government and policy makers'. Other categories like 'Infrastructure & resources' are implied but not explicitly named, and 'Private sector'/'Civil society' are absent.

TITLE: LA DIVERSIFICATION DE L'ENSEIGNEMENT POSTSECONDAIRE FACE A LA SITUATION DE L'KMPLOI Préambule
paragraph: 20. In order to achieve better coordination and integration between education, training, production, and research, it is desirable to facilitate, through appropriate measures, the establishment of cooperative relationships between postsecondary educational institutions, enterprises, and research establishments. These relationships should be mutually beneficial, particularly regarding the production, dissemination, and utilization of knowledge. Moreover, greater linkage between societal issues and the research themes of higher education should also be encouraged.

low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes collaboration between postsecondary institutions, research, and enterprises, focusing on knowledge production and integration. While vocational elements are mentioned, the primary emphasis is on academic research and systemic coordination, aligning with 'General (academic)'. 'Combined general and vocational' is excluded as the vocational aspects are secondary to the academic integration focus.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes systemic integration and coordination between education, enterprises, and research, aligning with 'Education system monitoring & evaluation' per the summary. However, this exact category isn't listed, so 'Other cross-cutting theme' is the closest fit. Other categories like 'Entrepreneurship' or 'Life skills' are not directly supported by the text.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph emphasizes collaboration between postsecondary institutions, enterprises, and research establishments, directly linking to 'Private sector and industry'. 'Government and policy makers' are implied through the call for 'appropriate measures' and systemic integration. Other categories like civil society or learners are not explicitly mentioned.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes coordination between education, research, and industry (governance/planning) and mentions knowledge production/dissemination (innovation/technology). 'Education system monitoring & evaluation' is excluded as the focus is on structural integration, not evaluation mechanisms.
low_level_thinking.Education system monitoring & evaluation: The paragraph emphasizes cooperative relationships and knowledge utilization, aligning with quality assurance through mutual benefit. Research and surveys are mentioned in the context of research themes and societal issues. Other categories like EMIS or institutional evaluation are not explicitly referenced.

TITLE: LA DIVERSIFICATION DE L'ENSEIGNEMENT POSTSECONDAIRE FACE A LA SITUATION DE L'KMPLOI Préambule
paragraph: Adequate material and financial resources should be ensured to support the proper development of infrastructure, laboratories, specialized equipment, and libraries so that staff and students remain motivated to promote quality education. In this regard, increased contributions from various socio-economic sectors would be desirable. Beyond seeking new funding sources, particular attention should be given to streamlining the processes of mobilizing, allocating, and utilizing available resources, as well as strengthening international and regional cooperation for the production of low-cost equipment and materials intended for teaching and research institutions. Existing institutions should be reinforced and modernized, rather than encouraging the proliferation of new ones.

low_level_thinking.Infrastructure & resources: The paragraph emphasizes infrastructure development, including laboratories and libraries, and mentions teaching materials. School buildings and physical infrastructure are directly referenced. Teaching and learning materials are explicitly mentioned. WASH, ICT, transport, and boarding are not directly addressed.
low_level_thinking.Ownership/Provision: The paragraph emphasizes streamlining resource mobilization and international cooperation, aligning with PPP's focus on collaborative resource management. 'Community or faith-based' is included due to the mention of 'community' in the context of institutional development. 'Public' and 'Private for-profit' are excluded as the text prioritizes collaboration over exclusive ownership models.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes resource mobilization and financing through funding sources and streamlining processes, aligning with 'Financing and resource mobilization'. Governance and planning are addressed through resource allocation, institutional modernization, and policy frameworks. International cooperation is explicitly mentioned in the context of producing low-cost equipment and materials, supporting 'International cooperation and partnerships'.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph emphasizes resource mobilization and allocation processes, which involve government policy frameworks. It mentions 'increased contributions from various socio-economic sectors' pointing to private sector involvement. International cooperation for low-cost equipment aligns with international organizations and donors. Learners/students are mentioned as beneficiaries but not as active stakeholders. Civil society/NGOs and communities are not explicitly referenced.

TITLE: LA DIVERSIFICATION DE L'ENSEIGNEMENT POSTSECONDAIRE FACE A LA SITUATION DE L'KMPLOI Préambule
paragraph: 22. For postsecondary education as a whole, it is essential to preserve university autonomy and academic freedoms, notably: the freedom of teaching and scientific research; freedom of expression, association, and publication; and free access to any publication. The exercise of these freedoms should be in accordance with the International Covenants on Economic, Social and Cultural Rights and on Civil and Political Rights adopted on 16 December 1966.

low_level_thinking.Legal frameworks: The paragraph references international human rights covenants (International Covenants on Economic, Social and Cultural Rights and on Civil and Political Rights), supporting 'International conventions and treaties'. It also mentions preserving academic freedoms aligned with fundamental rights, fitting 'Constitution and fundamental rights'. 'Policy implementation' is excluded as there's no mention of enforcement mechanisms.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes university autonomy and academic freedoms, aligning with governance structures (Governance and planning). Reference to international human rights covenants links to international legal frameworks and cooperation (International cooperation and partnerships). Other categories like Legal frameworks are excluded as they are not explicitly listed, and the focus is on policy implementation rather than pure legal norms.

TITLE: LA DIVERSIFICATION DE L'ENSEIGNEMENT POSTSECONDAIRE FACE A LA SITUATION DE L'KMPLOI Préambule
paragraph: 23. The evolution of the roles and functions of higher education institutions requires significant changes in their management. In this regard, it is desirable to modernize the management of postsecondary education institutions in order to enhance their efficiency and their capacity to use new techniques as well as improved equipment and methods that enable them to make rational decisions in a timely manner. Efforts should be intensified in ongoing research on how to make the management and administration of postsecondary education more effective.

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph focuses on modernizing management and administration of postsecondary institutions, aligning with 'Governance and planning'. Mention of new techniques, equipment, and methods supports 'Innovation and technology policies'. 'Quality and learning outcomes' is excluded as the emphasis is on management efficiency, not direct learning outcomes.
low_level_thinking.Education system monitoring & evaluation: The paragraph emphasizes modernizing management and improving efficiency, aligning with quality assurance and accreditation (modernization of institutions). It also mentions ongoing research on effective management, supporting 'Research and surveys'. 'Education statistics' and 'EMIS' are not explicitly mentioned. 'Institutional evaluation' is related but not directly addressed. 'Learning assessments' and 'Infrastructure' are not covered.
low_level_thinking.Infrastructure & resources: The paragraph mentions 'improved equipment and methods' which aligns with ICT and digital infrastructure. 'Teaching and learning materials' is supported by 'new techniques' and 'improved equipment'. Other categories like physical infrastructure or WASH are not explicitly mentioned. 'ICT and digital infrastructure' is directly tied to modernization efforts, while 'Teaching and learning materials' relates to enhanced resources for education.

TITLE: LA DIVERSIFICATION DE L'ENSEIGNEMENT POSTSECONDAIRE FACE A LA SITUATION DE L'KMPLOI Préambule
paragraph: 24. The diversification of postsecondary education, however timely and desirable, must under no circumstances result in lowering the standards of postsecondary teaching and training. In particular, caution should be exercised regarding the dangers of excessive fragmentation of vocational training, which may arise from a profusion of low-cost courses of poor quality purportedly designed to meet the "immediate needs" of job preparation. Attention must also be drawn to the risk that higher education, when relying on the industrial sector for financial support, may become subject to undue pressure from business leaders.

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes maintaining standards (Quality and learning outcomes) and risks of fragmentation and poor-quality courses. Governance and planning address systemic risks and fragmentation. Financing and resource mobilization relate to financial dependencies on the industrial sector.
low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph specifically addresses vocational training fragmentation and quality concerns, supporting 'Vocational/technical (TVET)'. The mention of higher education's financial reliance on industry and risks of business influence ties to broader education systems, justifying 'Combined general and vocational'. 'General (academic)' is excluded as the focus is on vocational and systemic risks, not purely academic standards.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph emphasizes the need for government oversight to maintain standards (implying government/policy role) and warns about private sector influence on higher education through financial support, aligning with 'Private sector and industry'. Other categories like educators or learners aren't directly mentioned.
low_level_thinking.Ownership/Provision: The paragraph highlights risks of financial dependency on the industrial sector, suggesting a mixed model where public education relies on private sector funding. This aligns with PPP characteristics. Other categories like 'Private for-profit' are excluded as the focus is on systemic risks, not direct profit motives. 'Public' alone is insufficient as the text implies collaboration with private entities.

TITLE: LA DIVERSIFICATION DE L'ENSEIGNEMENT POSTSECONDAIRE FACE A LA SITUATION DE L'KMPLOI Préambule
paragraph: 25. To successfully combat unemployment and underemployment among graduates, coordinated measures are essential from all concerned parties: government authorities, postsecondary educational institutions, public and private enterprises, and other societal organizations.

low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph explicitly mentions government authorities, private enterprises, and societal organizations, aligning with these categories. Teachers/learners/parents are not directly referenced. 'Civil society and NGOs' covers 'other societal organizations', while 'Private sector and industry' addresses enterprises. 'Government and policy makers' is explicitly named.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes coordinated measures among multiple stakeholders (government, institutions, enterprises), aligning with 'Stakeholder focus'. 'Governance and planning' reflects the need for structured collaboration. Other categories like 'Policy theme' are too broad, and 'Cross-cutting themes & skills' lacks explicit mention of skills.

TITLE: LA DIVERSIFICATION DE L'ENSEIGNEMENT POSTSECONDAIRE FACE A LA SITUATION DE L'KMPLOI Préambule
paragraph: 26. It is of fundamental importance, particularly in developing countries, that industrial or other investment projects related to postsecondary education, which involve new technologies or the use of new scientific processes or equipment, be carefully evaluated prior to their implementation, especially with regard to the practical conditions for the transfer of technology and scientific information, as well as the impact of the investment program on training and employment.

low_level_thinking.Education system monitoring & evaluation: The paragraph emphasizes careful evaluation of investment projects in postsecondary education, aligning with 'Quality assurance and accreditation' through technology transfer and impact assessment. 'Institutional evaluation and inspection' fits as it involves assessing practical conditions and training/employment impacts. 'Education statistics and indicators' and 'EMIS and data systems' are excluded as the focus is on evaluation processes, not data collection. 'Research and surveys' is less precise here.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes evaluation of technology-related investments (Innovation and technology policies), planning considerations for implementation (Governance and planning), and accountability through careful assessment of impacts (Accountability and transparency). 'Financing and resource mobilization' is excluded as the focus is on evaluation rather than funding mechanisms.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes technology transfer and scientific processes (aligning with Digital and ICT literacy). It also mentions employment impact and training, linking to Sustainable development through economic and social outcomes. 'Other cross-cutting theme' is used for broader implications like technology evaluation and policy considerations not fully captured by other categories.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph emphasizes evaluation of investment projects (private sector/industry) and policy considerations (government/policymakers) for technology transfer and employment impact. Civil society/NGOs and communities are not explicitly mentioned, while learners/teachers are not the focus here.

TITLE: LA DIVERSIFICATION DE L'ENSEIGNEMENT POSTSECONDAIRE FACE A LA SITUATION DE L'KMPLOI Préambule
paragraph: 27. It is important to improve the effectiveness of career information and guidance services. In particular, student information should be developed in order to better guide them towards the various streams of postsecondary education and to facilitate their choices. It is especially desirable that specialized services responsible for the integration of graduates into professional life at the end of their training be organized.

low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes career guidance and postsecondary education streams, aligning with 'General (academic)' for academic pathways. 'Vocational/technical (TVET)' is supported by mentions of professional integration and specialized services. 'Combined general and vocational' is excluded as the paragraph does not explicitly blend both areas but focuses on distinct aspects.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes improving the effectiveness of career guidance services and facilitating student choices, aligning with 'Quality and learning outcomes'. It also mentions organizing specialized services, which relates to 'Governance and planning'. Categories like 'Curriculum and pedagogy policies' and 'Inclusion and diversity' were excluded as the text does not directly address curriculum or diversity issues.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph emphasizes guiding students towards postsecondary education and facilitating their choices, directly relating to learners and students. It also mentions specialized services for graduate integration, implying educators' role in career guidance. Government and policy makers are not explicitly mentioned, and other categories like parents or private sector are not directly referenced.

TITLE: LA DIVERSIFICATION DE L'ENSEIGNEMENT POSTSECONDAIRE FACE A LA SITUATION DE L'KMPLOI Préambule
paragraph: The rapid obsolescence of knowledge and techniques requires that postsecondary education offer various types of continuing education and retraining courses. Postsecondary education institutions should provide all individuals who already hold a basic university degree or professional qualification the opportunity to retrain, specialize, or change careers without having to interrupt their professional activities. They should also offer those who have completed their professional careers and all adults with free time the possibility to remain connected with the world of science, new technologies, and culture, enabling them to continue actively participating in the development of their society.

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes retraining, career changes, and staying connected to science/technology, aligning with 'Lifelong learning and adult education'. 'Innovation and technology policies' is included due to references to new technologies. Other categories like 'Access and equity' or 'Governance and planning' lack direct evidence.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes lifelong learning and adaptability (Life skills), mentions science and new technologies (Digital literacy), and implies societal development through active participation (Sustainable development). Other categories like citizenship or human rights are not explicitly addressed.
low_level_thinking.Learner population: The paragraph emphasizes retraining for individuals with existing degrees, career changers, and adults seeking to stay engaged with science and culture, aligning with 'Adults and lifelong learners'. Other categories like 'Children' or 'Youth' are excluded as the focus is on post-degree and career-stage adults. 'Lifelong learning' is explicitly framed, supporting this selection over broader or less relevant categories.
low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes both academic retraining (general) and vocational retraining (vocational/technical) for career changes and societal participation. 'Combined' is chosen as it explicitly mentions both retraining and staying connected to science/technology, aligning with lifelong learning themes in the summary.

TITLE: LA DIVERSIFICATION DE L'ENSEIGNEMENT POSTSECONDAIRE FACE A LA SITUATION DE L'KMPLOI Préambule
paragraph: 29. It is appropriate that the knowledge acquired through professional development and continuing education be officially certified in order to allow, without discrimination, those who have benefited from it to potentially improve their position and mobility in the labor market based on their new qualifications.

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes certification of professional development for labor market mobility, aligning with lifelong learning. Non-discrimination and equity in access to qualifications support 'Access and equity'. 'Legal frameworks' is excluded as certification is not framed as legal but as policy. 'Education orientation' and 'Learner population' are less central here.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes non-discrimination and labor market mobility, aligning with 'Human rights and equality'. 'Life skills and 21st century competencies' fits as certification enhances employability skills. 'Other cross-cutting theme' was excluded due to specific mentions of equity and skills.
low_level_thinking.Legal frameworks: The paragraph discusses certification of professional development, aligning with education laws/regulations. Non-discrimination in labor market mobility ties to stakeholders' rights/obligations. Other categories lack direct evidence from the paragraph.

TITLE: LA DIVERSIFICATION DE L'ENSEIGNEMENT POSTSECONDAIRE FACE A LA SITUATION DE L'KMPLOI Préambule
paragraph: 30. It is essential to strengthen the links between postsecondary education and the community and society it serves, particularly with regard to: (a) the promotion of fundamental and applied research as well as advisory services; (b) participation in national development efforts; (c) expanding adult access to various forms of education; (d) involvement in efforts to democratize education, with particular attention to disadvantaged groups; (e) improving the practical training of students within businesses; (f) the ongoing updating and enhancement of the qualifications of postsecondary education staff.

low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes both academic research (general) and practical training (vocational), with references to staff qualifications and community links. 'Combined general and vocational' captures the dual focus, while 'General (academic)' addresses research and development aspects. 'Vocational/technical' is excluded as the vocational elements are not the primary focus.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph emphasizes expanding adult access to education (learners/students), improving practical training within businesses (private sector), updating qualifications of staff (teachers/educators), and community links (parents/communities). Cross-cutting themes like democratizing education and national development efforts imply stakeholder engagement beyond traditional categories.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes expanding adult education (Lifelong learning), inclusion of disadvantaged groups (Inclusion and diversity), equitable access (Access and equity), and improving practical training/qualifications (Quality). 'Curriculum' and 'pedagogy' are not explicitly mentioned, so they are excluded. 'Policy theme' is the high-level label, so sub-labels focus on specific aspects within it.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes democratizing education for disadvantaged groups (human rights/equality), inclusive access for adults (inclusive education), and practical training/qualification enhancement (life skills). 'Citizenship' and 'sustainable development' are less directly supported, while 'entrepreneurship' and 'digital literacy' are not explicitly mentioned.
low_level_thinking.Learner population: The paragraph explicitly mentions expanding adult access to education (Adults and lifelong learners) and improving practical training within businesses, which aligns with lifelong learning. 'Disadvantaged groups' is directly referenced in the context of democratizing education. 'Indigenous or minority groups' is implied through the mention of 'disadvantaged groups' and the cross-cutting theme of equity. Other categories like 'Children' or 'Youth' are not explicitly mentioned, and 'Women and girls' lack direct evidence.

TITLE: LA DIVERSIFICATION DE L'ENSEIGNEMENT POSTSECONDAIRE FACE A LA SITUATION DE L'KMPLOI Préambule
paragraph: 31. In terms of research, post-secondary educational institutions, and particularly universities, have a significant role to play. They are called upon to make a major contribution to the training of researchers. Since research, especially fundamental research, is often very costly, these institutions should pool their resources, whether human or otherwise, within the framework of cooperative projects.

low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph focuses on post-secondary institutions and universities' role in research training, aligning with 'General (academic)'. 'Combined general and vocational' is excluded as there's no mention of vocational skills. 'Vocational/technical (TVET)' is not supported by the text, which emphasizes research over technical training.
low_level_thinking.Infrastructure & resources: The paragraph emphasizes collaborative resource pooling for research, which includes laboratories and libraries as key research facilities. ICT infrastructure is implied through mentions of cooperative projects requiring shared resources. Other categories like classrooms or transport are not explicitly mentioned.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph emphasizes universities' role in training researchers, aligning with 'Teachers and educators'. 'Other' is included due to the focus on collaborative resource pooling, which doesn't fit neatly into existing categories. 'Government and policy makers' and 'Private sector and industry' are excluded as the text doesn't mention policy or industry involvement.

TITLE: LA DIVERSIFICATION DE L'ENSEIGNEMENT POSTSECONDAIRE FACE A LA SITUATION DE L'KMPLOI Préambule
paragraph: Measures should be taken to prevent all forms of discrimination, including any distinction, exclusion, limitation, or preference based on race, color, sex, language, religion, political or other opinion, national or social origin, economic status, or birth, which aims to destroy or impair equality of treatment in the field of education. To this end, it is essential to implement measures to ensure that students who meet the required criteria and whose economic situation necessitates it are granted scholarships, loans, or other forms of social and financial assistance. Special attention should be given to vocational training for women and girls. Accordingly, the enrollment of girls in scientific and technical disciplines should be encouraged. Greater emphasis should also be placed on recruiting female professors in postsecondary education.

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes anti-discrimination (Inclusion and diversity) and equity in education access (Access and equity). Gender-specific measures like vocational training for women and recruiting female professors fall under Teachers and workforce policies. TITLE: LA DIVERSIFICATION DE L'ENSEIGNEMENT POSTSECONDAIRE FACE A LA SITUATION DE L'KMPLOI Préambule
paragraph: Distance education, correspondence courses, evening classes, as well as other forms of part-time postsecondary education should be encouraged and diversified to better meet the educational and training needs of various population groups, particularly adult workers. Special attention should be given to improving the quality of this education.

The focus on adult workers and meeting diverse needs supports 'Lifelong learning'. 'Formal education' is excluded as the text emphasizes non-traditional formats. 'Informal learning' is not explicitly mentioned.
low_level_thinking.Learner population: The paragraph explicitly mentions 'adult workers' which directly maps to 'Adults and lifelong learners'. 'Low-income and disadvantaged groups' is inferred as 'various population groups' may include economically disadvantaged individuals. Other categories like 'Children' or 'Youth' are excluded as the focus is on adult learners.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph explicitly mentions part-time postsecondary education and adult workers, aligning with 'Lifelong learning and adult education'. It also emphasizes improving quality, supporting 'Quality and learning outcomes'. Other categories like 'Inclusion and diversity' or 'Policy theme' are not directly supported by the specific mention of education formats and quality improvement.

TITLE: LA DIVERSIFICATION DE L'ENSEIGNEMENT POSTSECONDAIRE FACE A LA SITUATION DE L'KMPLOI Préambule
paragraph: 34. Postsecondary education should make all students aware of the major current and future issues faced today by national and international communities, such as violations of human rights, the arms race, racism, the deterioration of the physical and natural environment and cultural heritage, the persistence in most parts of the world of poverty, hunger, malnutrition, illiteracy, and all forms of exploitation, and should stimulate their willingness to contribute effectively to their resolution.

low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph specifies content areas (human rights, environment, poverty) and learning outcomes (stimulating willingness to contribute to solutions), fitting 'Content and learning areas' and 'Competency-based and learning outcomes'. 'Curriculum implementation' and 'evaluation' are not mentioned, while 'design' and 'organization' are not explicitly addressed.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes global challenges like human rights violations, environmental degradation, and poverty, aligning with Global citizenship and peace education. Human rights and equality directly address mentioned issues like racism and exploitation. Sustainable development and climate action cover environmental concerns. Other categories lack direct textual support.
low_level_thinking.Subject domain: The paragraph emphasizes global issues like human rights, poverty, and environmental degradation, aligning with social sciences and humanities. Environmental education is explicitly mentioned. Other categories like STEM or vocational skills are not addressed.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes curriculum content addressing global issues (Curriculum and pedagogy policies), mentions environmental and social challenges (Sustainable development and environment), and touches on diversity through references to racism and exploitation (Inclusion and diversity). Governance, financing, and other categories lack direct textual support.

TITLE: LA DIVERSIFICATION DE L'ENSEIGNEMENT POSTSECONDAIRE FACE A LA SITUATION DE L'KMPLOI Préambule
paragraph: 35. Given the increasing importance of diversifying postsecondary education in response to the new employment situation, socio-economic changes, and the continuous challenges posed by advances in science and technology, a coordinated regional and international approach is essential to assist Member States in better fulfilling their responsibilities regarding the development and improvement of this education.

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph mentions 'advances in science and technology' supporting 'Innovation and technology policies'. 'Coordinated regional and international approach' aligns with 'International cooperation and partnerships'. 'Diversifying postsecondary education' and 'socio-economic changes' relate to 'Inclusion and diversity'. Other categories like 'Governance and planning' or 'Financing' are not explicitly addressed.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph emphasizes coordinated regional and international approaches to support Member States, pointing to government roles in policy and international organizations in coordination. Other stakeholders like educators or learners are not explicitly mentioned as primary actors in this context.

TITLE: LA DIVERSIFICATION DE L'ENSEIGNEMENT POSTSECONDAIRE FACE A LA SITUATION DE L'KMPLOI Préambule
paragraph: 36. Member States should take measures to foster an intellectual climate and material conditions conducive to the mobility of teachers, students, researchers, and members of intellectual professions, and to help overcome the difficulties encountered by those returning to their home countries after studying abroad.

'Teacher supply and shortages' addresses mobility barriers affecting availability, while 'Working conditions and salaries' covers material conditions. Other categories like 'Recruitment and hiring' or 'Professional development' are less directly supported by the text.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph explicitly mentions teachers, students, and researchers, directly aligning with 'Teachers and educators' and 'Learners and students'. 'Government and policy makers' is excluded as the focus is on individuals rather than policymakers. 'Civil society' and 'private sector' are not mentioned, and 'international organizations' are not explicitly referenced.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes international mobility and collaboration, aligning with 'Global citizenship and peace education' through cross-border academic movement. 'Life skills and 21st century competencies' fits as it addresses overcoming challenges in mobility, a key 21st-century skill. Other categories like 'Infrastructure & resources' are excluded as the text focuses on policy and skills, not physical resources.

TITLE: LA DIVERSIFICATION DE L'ENSEIGNEMENT POSTSECONDAIRE FACE A LA SITUATION DE L'KMPLOI Préambule
paragraph: 37. Member States should continue to strengthen and develop bilateral, regional, and international cooperation in order to better adapt postsecondary education to socio-economic needs and to provide all necessary support to higher education centers established by the United Nations and UNESCO, to interuniversity cooperation networks for the continuing education of higher education personnel, as well as to regional educational innovation programs established by UNESCO, insofar as these programs apply to postsecondary education.

Vocational/technical (TVET) is excluded as the text does not mention specific vocational skills or technical training. Combined general and vocational is not selected because the paragraph does not clearly integrate both general and vocational aspects.

Mention of continuing education and postsecondary adaptation aligns with lifelong learning. 'Policy theme' was debated but not selected. 'Access and equity' and 'Quality' are not explicitly addressed.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph emphasizes cooperation between Member States (government/policy makers) and international entities like UNESCO, indicating stakeholder focus. 'International organizations and donors' is directly referenced through UNESCO's role. Other categories like educators or learners are not explicitly mentioned, so they are excluded.

TITLE: LA DIVERSIFICATION DE L'ENSEIGNEMENT POSTSECONDAIRE FACE A LA SITUATION DE L'KMPLOI Préambule
paragraph: 38. Due to the diversity of experiences gained, Member States should initiate or develop, on a subregional, regional, or interregional basis, the essential institutional cooperation for the implementation and strengthening of forms of diversification relevant to addressing socio-economic development issues.

'International cooperation and partnerships' fits due to the cross-border collaboration mentioned. Other categories like 'Sustainable development and environment' or 'Education system monitoring & evaluation' are less directly supported by the text.
