TITLE: LA CONTRIBUTION DE L'’EDUCATION AU DEVELOPPEMENT CULTUREL
paragraph: Recalling United Nations General Assembly resolution No. 41/187, which declared the period 1988-1997 as the World Decade for Cultural Development, with an Action Programme structured around four main objectives: taking into account the cultural dimension in development, affirming and enriching cultural identities, broadening participation in cultural life, and promoting international cultural cooperation,

TITLE: LA CONTRIBUTION DE L'’EDUCATION AU DEVELOPPEMENT CULTUREL
paragraph: 1. Culture: The definition adopted by the World Conference on Cultural Policies (Mexico, 1982) considers culture as "the set of distinctive spiritual and material, intellectual and emotional features that characterize a society or social group." It encompasses lifestyles, traditions and beliefs, the arts and literature, while integrating fundamental human rights into its value system. The culture of a country is not limited to learned culture; it also includes popular culture. It is not confined to heritage but is enriched and developed both through creativity and memory. To remain alive, it cannot turn inward. It is through exchanges that it is fertilized. Finally, scientific culture constitutes an increasingly important part of humanity’s culture, to which it contributes by establishing universality.

low_level_thinking.Subject domain: The paragraph centers on defining culture, including arts, literature, traditions, and heritage, aligning with 'Arts and culture'. It also discusses societal values, human rights, and cultural integration, fitting 'Social sciences and humanities'. Other categories like STEM or literacy are not mentioned, and 'Policy theme' is excluded as the focus is definitional, not strategic.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph explicitly integrates 'fundamental human rights' into the cultural value system, directly aligning with 'Human rights and equality'. The broader cultural definition and emphasis on inclusivity (e.g., popular culture, scientific culture) justify 'Other cross-cutting theme' as it captures cross-cutting educational relevance beyond specific domains. Categories like 'Global citizenship' or 'Digital literacy' are less directly supported by the text.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes cultural diversity and inclusion by defining culture as encompassing all societal groups and highlighting the importance of exchanges. 'International cooperation and partnerships' is supported by the mention of cross-cultural fertilization and global scientific culture. 'Inclusion and diversity' aligns with integrating diverse cultural elements and human rights. Other categories like 'Governance and planning' or 'Legal frameworks' are less directly supported by the text.

TITLE: LA CONTRIBUTION DE L'’EDUCATION AU DEVELOPPEMENT CULTUREL
paragraph: 2. Development: This term denotes both an evolution and a change of state, progress, enrichment, or fulfillment. It is neither solely nor primarily measured by the increase in the quantity of goods and therefore cannot be equated with, nor reduced to, material growth. Development must be assessed as much by the improvement it brings to the richness of the human being as by the quality of their life. Nowadays, it is recognized that the cultural dimension constitutes an essential aspect of any policy for socio-economic, technological, scientific, or other forms of development.

low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes the cultural dimension as an essential aspect of development, which aligns with the 'Other cross-cutting theme' category. Other categories like 'Sustainable development and climate action' or 'Human rights and equality' are not directly mentioned. The debate summary supports treating cultural aspects as cross-cutting, justifying the selection over more specific categories.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes cultural dimension as essential to development, aligning with 'Inclusion and diversity' through recognition of cultural aspects. 'Sustainable development and environment' is chosen due to the mention of development encompassing quality of life and enrichment, which relates to sustainable development goals. Other categories like 'Cross-cutting themes & skills' are excluded as per the summary's context and the paragraph's focus on policy theme aspects.

TITLE: LA CONTRIBUTION DE L'’EDUCATION AU DEVELOPPEMENT CULTUREL
paragraph: 3. Cultural development: Cultural development as a dynamic process means the enrichment of culture, the strengthening of forms of cultural expression, and the extension of culture to everyone by promoting broad participation and creativity through appropriate means in a spirit of mutual respect and tolerance.

low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes cultural development through mutual respect, tolerance, and inclusive participation, which aligns with 'Other cross-cutting theme' as it captures the unique focus on cultural enrichment and accessibility. Other categories like 'Human rights and equality' or 'Inclusive education and special needs' are too narrow or specific to fit the broader cultural development context.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes mutual respect, tolerance, and broad participation, aligning with 'Inclusion and diversity'. Other categories like 'Governance and planning' or 'International cooperation' lack direct evidence.

TITLE: LA CONTRIBUTION DE L'’EDUCATION AU DEVELOPPEMENT CULTUREL
paragraph: 4. Education: Implementation of a process of comprehensive human development that occurs throughout life and aims at the fullest possible development intellectually, physically, emotionally, morally, and spiritually. Educational actions have the duty not only to transmit knowledge, skills, values, and other components of the culture to which the individual belongs, but also to promote their autonomy and creativity. By fostering an open and discerning mind, education increases the individual’s capacity to enrich both their own culture and others’. It also contributes to a better integration of the individual into cultural, social, and economic life and, accordingly, to the formation of human resources upon which all moral and material development depends. Regardless of the diverse paths it may take, education must maintain dynamic interactive relations with culture. To effectively fulfill its role as an agent of cultural development and better prepare individuals for life in a society enriched by multiple cultures, education requires freedom of expression, freedom of participation, free exchanges among people, the benefits of science, and cultural creations. It requires equal access to the learning process as well as a democratic cultural life of which it is one of the principal means of creation and improvement.

low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes holistic human development, intellectual and moral growth, and cultural integration, aligning with general academic education. Vocational/technical categories are excluded as the text focuses on broad cultural and personal development rather than specific skills or TVET. Combined categories are not selected due to lack of explicit vocational references.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes autonomy, creativity, and democratic cultural life, aligning with life skills and 21st century competencies. Human rights and equality are addressed through freedom of expression and equal access. Inclusive education is implied by the duty to transmit culture and promote integration, supporting special needs inclusion.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes lifelong development and holistic education, aligning with 'Lifelong learning and adult education'. 'Inclusion and diversity' is supported by references to multiple cultures and equal access. 'Curriculum and pedagogy policies' relates to fostering open minds and cultural interactions, though the text does not explicitly mention curriculum design, it frames education's role in shaping cultural and intellectual development.
low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph emphasizes comprehensive human development and the content of education (intellectual, physical, emotional, etc.), supporting 'Content and learning areas'. It discusses the design of educational processes and their relationship with culture, aligning with 'Design and development'. 'Curriculum implementation' is directly addressed through mentions of dynamic relations with culture, freedom of expression, and access to learning. 'Curriculum evaluation and reform' is excluded as there's no mention of assessment or reform. 'Competency-based' is not explicitly framed. 'Other' is avoided due to specificity.
low_level_thinking.Pedagogy & assessment: The paragraph emphasizes the foundational role of education in holistic development, aligning with 'Pedagogical foundations and theories'. It also mentions the need for assessment through the promotion of autonomy and creativity, which ties to 'Assessment and feedback'. Other categories like teaching methods or technology are not explicitly mentioned, and the focus on cultural integration and freedom does not fit the other pedagogy-related categories.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph emphasizes education as a process involving teachers (fostering open minds, promoting autonomy), learners (enriching culture, integrating into society), and parents/communities (cultural transmission, democratic life). Civil society/NGOs and international actors are not explicitly mentioned, while policy makers are implied but not directly referenced.

TITLE: LA CONTRIBUTION DE L'’EDUCATION AU DEVELOPPEMENT CULTUREL
paragraph: 5. Education in culture: This term encompasses: (a) the initiation into the knowledge and appreciation of cultural heritage as well as the introduction to contemporary cultural life; (b) the familiarization with the processes of cultural dissemination and evolution; (c) the recognition of their equal dignity as well as the indissoluble link binding cultural heritage and contemporary culture; (d) artistic and aesthetic education; (e) training in ethical and civic values; (f) media education; (g) intercultural/multicultural education.

low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph outlines cultural heritage, artistic education, ethical values, and media education, which align with general academic orientation. Vocational/technical categories are excluded as the focus is on cultural and ethical education rather than job-specific skills. Combined categories are not selected due to lack of explicit vocational components.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph includes 'training in ethical and civic values' (Citizenship and civic education), 'media education' (Digital and ICT literacy), 'intercultural/multicultural education' (Inclusive education and special needs), and broader cultural education themes not fitting other categories (Other cross-cutting theme). 'Global citizenship' and 'Human rights' are not explicitly mentioned, while 'life skills' and 'sustainability' are not directly referenced.
low_level_thinking.Subject domain: The paragraph explicitly mentions 'artistic and aesthetic education' (Arts and culture), 'ethical and civic values' (Religion and moral education), and references cultural heritage and contemporary culture (Social sciences and humanities). 'Other subject area' was excluded as specific categories are clearly delineated.

TITLE: LA CONTRIBUTION DE L'’EDUCATION AU DEVELOPPEMENT CULTUREL
paragraph: 6. Interculturality/Multiculturalism: These terms refer to the knowledge and appreciation of different cultures and the establishment of positive relationships of exchange and mutual enrichment among cultural components within a country as well as between the diverse cultures of the world. Contemporary societies, owing to a variety of factors specific to the modern world, face the multicultural phenomenon to varying degrees. Given this trend toward greater cultural diversity, multiculturalism presupposes surpassing both assimilationism and the passive coexistence of multiple cultures, while fostering self-respect alongside respect for and understanding of the cultures of others.

low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph focuses on interculturality and multiculturalism, emphasizing cultural diversity, mutual respect, and cross-cultural understanding. These themes align with broader societal values and cross-cutting principles rather than specific educational skills or domains. Other categories like Global citizenship and peace education are partially relevant but do not capture the core focus on cultural exchange and mutual enrichment as precisely.

TITLE: LA CONTRIBUTION DE L'’EDUCATION AU DEVELOPPEMENT CULTUREL
paragraph: 7. Intercultural/multicultural education: Addressed to all students and citizens, it aims to promote respect for cultural diversity, as well as mutual understanding and enrichment. Genuine intercultural/multicultural pedagogy should not be limited to additional content nor relegated to ancillary school activities or specific subjects; it must permeate all teaching and the entire educational structure. This education involves the joint responsibility of educators and all relevant partners, including families, cultural institutions, and the media. It generally encompasses programs, courses, or activities aimed at all learners that seek, within an educational context, to promote respect for cultural diversity and enhance understanding of the cultures of different identifiable groups. Moreover, it fosters integration and academic success, improves international understanding, and combats all forms of exclusion. The objective should be to move from understanding one’s own culture to appreciating the cultures of neighboring peoples and, ultimately, to that of world culture.

low_level_thinking.Pedagogy & assessment: The paragraph emphasizes foundational principles of intercultural pedagogy (Pedagogical foundations and theories), shared responsibility among multiple stakeholders (Stakeholder focus), and the overarching goal of promoting cultural understanding (Education orientation). 'Assessment and feedback' is excluded as there's no mention of evaluation methods. 'Policy theme' is debated but not directly addressed, and 'Curriculum' is too narrow compared to the broader 'Education orientation'.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes intercultural/multicultural education as a cross-cutting theme permeating all teaching, aligning with 'Other cross-cutting theme'. It mentions inclusion of diverse cultures and combating exclusion, fitting 'Inclusive education and special needs'. Global citizenship is addressed through promoting mutual understanding, international awareness, and cultural appreciation, matching 'Global citizenship and peace education'.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph explicitly mentions shared responsibility among educators, families, cultural institutions, and media, supporting 'Stakeholder focus'. 'Education orientation' captures the goal of promoting cultural diversity. 'Pedagogy & assessment' and 'Cross-cutting themes & skills' align with the normative pedagogical framework and cross-cutting goals. 'Policy theme' is debated but not definitively included, while 'Legal frameworks' is excluded due to lack of legal references.
low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes intercultural/multicultural education as a core pedagogical approach permeating all teaching and the educational structure, aligning with 'General (academic)'. 'Combined general and vocational' is excluded as there's no vocational focus. 'Vocational/technical (TVET)' is irrelevant. 'Stakeholder focus' from the summary is not an allowed category, and 'Policy theme' is debated but not selected here.

TITLE: LA CONTRIBUTION DE L'’EDUCATION AU DEVELOPPEMENT CULTUREL
paragraph: 8. Science, technology, and culture: The progress of all contemporary societies is profoundly shaped by science and technology, whose development is strongly influenced by social and cultural factors. In order to be truly free and to overcome the constraints of its natural and social environment, human beings need science and technology. However, for science and technology to be liberating, scientific and technological culture must be connected to ethical and humanistic values. The ultimate goal remains the flourishing and future of the human being, that is to say, each individual who constitutes the human community.

low_level_thinking.Subject domain: The paragraph emphasizes science, technology, and their cultural/ethical connections, supporting STEM and Social sciences/humanities. 'Arts and culture' is included due to mention of cultural factors and humanistic values. 'Other subject area' is excluded as the focus is explicitly on science/tech and their integration with cultural/ethical dimensions.

TITLE: LA CONTRIBUTION DE L'’EDUCATION AU DEVELOPPEMENT CULTUREL
paragraph: 9. Coordination of education and culture development policies with those of development: Member States are called upon to strengthen this coordination. To better integrate education and culture, as well as both with development, the educational and cultural policies of Member States may be formulated and implemented with the participation and cooperation of other societal institutions that contribute to cultural and socio-economic development. These policies may be based on the following considerations: (a) strengthening cooperation between formal and non-formal educational institutions and cultural institutions; (b) increasing the participation of cultural institution personnel in the educational process; (c) expanding the network of cultural establishments, coordinated with the improvement of initial and ongoing training for the personnel of this network; (d) strengthening educational programs delivered through the media; (e) taking into account, in the allocation of public and private resources, the mutual needs of education and culture.

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes coordination between education, culture, and development policies (Governance and planning). It mentions participation of societal institutions (International cooperation and partnerships). Inclusion and diversity is implied through 'mutual needs of education and culture' and cooperation across sectors.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes coordination between education, culture, and development policies, involving societal institutions and resource allocation. This aligns with 'Other cross-cutting theme' as it lacks specific focus on listed categories like digital literacy or human rights. low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes integration of education and culture with development, suggesting academic (general) focus. 'Combined general and vocational' is supported by mentions of training and media-based education programs, which blend academic and vocational elements. 'Vocational/technical (TVET)' is excluded as the text does not specifically prioritize technical skills or TVET.
low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph emphasizes designing policies that integrate education and culture, aligning with 'Design and development'. It also discusses implementation through institutional cooperation and media programs, fitting 'Curriculum implementation'. 'Content and learning areas' is excluded as the focus is on policy coordination, not specific content. 'Curriculum evaluation and reform' is not directly addressed.
low_level_thinking.Pedagogy & assessment: The paragraph emphasizes integrating education and culture through institutional cooperation and policy formulation, aligning with pedagogical foundations. It mentions collaboration with societal institutions and training for personnel, supporting professional learning communities. Other categories like teaching methods or technology are not explicitly addressed.
low_level_thinking.Subject domain: The paragraph emphasizes integration of education and culture with development, explicitly mentioning cultural institutions, media-based education, and societal institutions. 'Arts and culture' directly aligns with cultural institutions and networks. 'Social sciences and humanities' covers policy coordination, stakeholder involvement, and curriculum considerations mentioned in the summary. Other categories like 'Environmental education' or 'Vocational skills' are not explicitly referenced.
low_level_thinking.Legal frameworks: The paragraph discusses formulation and implementation of educational and cultural policies, aligning with 'Education laws and regulations' and 'Policy implementation and enforcement'. It also mentions the participation and cooperation of societal institutions, supporting 'Rights and obligations of stakeholders'. Categories like 'Constitution and fundamental rights' and 'International conventions and treaties' are not directly addressed. 'Legal disputes and interpretation' and 'Other' are excluded due to lack of evidence.

TITLE: LA CONTRIBUTION DE L'’EDUCATION AU DEVELOPPEMENT CULTUREL
paragraph: 10. The Role of the School in Promoting Culture: To better meet the demands of contemporary societies and the challenges of the twenty-first century, the school must open itself to its social, economic, and cultural environment and become a place of fruitful dialogue by broadening its horizon to include the cultures of the world, whether regional, national, or local. Beyond the development of intelligence, powers of observation, critical reasoning, and problem-solving skills, the school must achieve a balance between the intellectual and analytical qualities and the affective, spiritual, and moral qualities of learners. It must become more responsive to the needs of the community and society as a whole.

low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes 21st-century skills like critical reasoning and problem-solving, aligning with 'Life skills and 21st century competencies'. 'Global citizenship and peace education' matches the call for cultural dialogue and societal engagement. 'Inclusive education and special needs' fits the balance of affective and intellectual qualities. Other categories like citizenship or digital literacy lack direct textual support.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes broadening curricular horizons to include global cultures (Curriculum and pedagogy policies), promoting diverse cultural engagement (Inclusion and diversity), and aligning education with societal needs (Accountability and transparency). 'Policy theme' is excluded as the focus is on implementation rather than overarching policy frameworks.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph emphasizes the school's role in engaging with communities and society, aligning with 'Parents and communities'. It discusses the development of learners' qualities, supporting 'Learners and students'. Teachers are implied as key actors in fostering dialogue and skills, justifying 'Teachers and educators'.

TITLE: LA CONTRIBUTION DE L'’EDUCATION AU DEVELOPPEMENT CULTUREL
paragraph: 11. Development of interdisciplinary and cooperative activities: The teaching of various disciplines should lead to interdisciplinary and cooperative work aimed at promoting cultural and intercultural education. To achieve better integration of cultural, intercultural, and interdisciplinary practices, the institution would benefit from adopting a project-based approach. The project is intended to encourage teamwork around common objectives. Organizing cooperation with artistic and cultural partners through twinning arrangements between educational and cultural institutions could enhance the effectiveness of teaching.

low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes teamwork, project-based learning, and interdisciplinary cooperation, aligning with 'Life skills and 21st century competencies'. 'Other cross-cutting theme' is used as the paragraph discusses cultural, intercultural, and interdisciplinary practices not fully captured by existing categories. Categories like 'Global citizenship' or 'Sustainable development' are excluded as the text focuses on pedagogical approaches rather than specific thematic goals.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph emphasizes collaboration between educational institutions and cultural partners (implying teachers/educators), mentions twinning arrangements with cultural institutions (suggesting community involvement), and references artistic/cultural partners which aligns with civil society/NGOs. Government and policy makers are not explicitly mentioned, while private sector and international organizations are not directly referenced.
low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph emphasizes designing interdisciplinary activities and project-based approaches (Design and development). It also discusses implementing cooperative work and partnerships (Curriculum implementation). 'Content and learning areas' is excluded as the focus is on methods rather than specific content. 'Curriculum evaluation and reform' is not mentioned. 'Competency-based' is not explicitly framed.
low_level_thinking.Pedagogy & assessment: The paragraph emphasizes project-based learning and cooperative work, aligning with 'Teaching methods and approaches'. Twinning arrangements and collaboration with cultural partners support 'Professional learning communities'. 'Pedagogical foundations and theories' is less precise here. 'Assessment and feedback' is not directly addressed. Other categories are not supported by the text.

TITLE: LA CONTRIBUTION DE L'’EDUCATION AU DEVELOPPEMENT CULTUREL
paragraph: 12. Taking into account the cultural and intercultural dimension in the curricula: It is essential to recognize the role of culture as the foundation upon which the content of education must be built. Consequently, the curricula of various disciplines, including scientific and technological fields, should be developed with the participation of specialists in education sciences, cultural matters, as well as anthropologists, sociologists, psychologists, and others.

low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph emphasizes the involvement of specialists in curriculum design (Design and development) and the integration of cultural content into various disciplines (Content and learning areas). 'Curriculum implementation' and 'evaluation' are not directly addressed. 'Competency-based' and 'cross-cutting' themes are mentioned in the summary but not explicitly in the paragraph.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes cultural integration in curricula as a transversal competency, aligning with 'Other cross-cutting theme' as it is not explicitly covered by other categories. Categories like 'Citizenship' or 'Human rights' are not directly mentioned. The orchestrator summary notes the debate on categorization under cross-cutting themes, supporting this selection.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes curriculum development with cultural integration, aligning with 'Curriculum and pedagogy policies'. It also highlights inclusion of diverse cultural perspectives, fitting 'Inclusion and diversity'. Other categories like 'Cross-cutting themes & skills' are not explicitly labeled here, and the focus remains on curriculum and cultural diversity.

TITLE: LA CONTRIBUTION DE L'’EDUCATION AU DEVELOPPEMENT CULTUREL
paragraph: 13. Introduction to the understanding and appreciation of cultural heritage: The educational presentation of heritage, which relies on various materials such as textbooks, guides, and audiovisual documents, must be accompanied, in collaboration with cultural institutions, by visits to cultural establishments, sites, and monuments, as well as practical activities. This presentation constitutes the cultural foundation upon which each people base their unique identity and their contribution to human civilization. This appreciation of cultural heritage should encourage students to ask questions that go beyond the mere material dimension of culture, addressing the symbolic value and meaning of this heritage, considered in relation to the contemporary world and universal values.

low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph emphasizes content related to cultural heritage education (Content and learning areas) and collaboration with institutions along with practical activities (Curriculum implementation). 'Competency-based' and 'evaluation' are not explicitly mentioned, and 'stakeholder focus' is partially addressed but not the primary emphasis.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes critical thinking and questioning beyond material dimensions, aligning with life skills and 21st century competencies. 'Other cross-cutting theme' is used as the content addresses cultural heritage integration, which isn't fully captured by existing categories. Citizenship and civic education is excluded as the focus is on cultural appreciation, not civic duties.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph emphasizes collaboration with cultural institutions (implying educators' role), mentions students as recipients of cultural education, and references community-based heritage appreciation, aligning with learners/students and parents/communities. Government and others are not explicitly mentioned.

TITLE: LA CONTRIBUTION DE L'’EDUCATION AU DEVELOPPEMENT CULTUREL
paragraph: 14. Teaching and languages: The choice of one or more languages, whether native, national, or foreign, as a subject of study or as mediums for other disciplines is determined by the educational and cultural policy of each country. When selecting the language of instruction, particularly at the fundamental education level, both the effectiveness of the educational process and the right of individuals and different ethnic groups to preserve their cultural identity, of which language is one of the most important vehicles, should be taken into account. The views of these groups should be considered as fully as possible when decisions concerning them are made. It is desirable that the educational system, notably through extracurricular cultural and educational activities, contributes to the preservation and development of minority languages, especially those that are unwritten. In this perspective, research should be conducted in fields such as pedagogy, linguistics, anthropology, and history, and efforts should be encouraged to develop teaching materials in the mother tongue.

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph discusses language selection as a curriculum decision (Curriculum and pedagogy policies), emphasizes cultural identity and minority languages (Inclusion and diversity), and mentions policy determination by each country (Governance and planning). Excluded categories like 'Education in emergencies' or 'Health' are not relevant here.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes cultural identity preservation (human rights), minority language support (inclusive education), and cross-cutting themes like language policy. 'Other cross-cutting theme' is included due to the policy and cultural framing. 'Global citizenship' and 'curriculum' were excluded as the focus is on language policy and rights, not global citizenship or explicit curriculum design.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph emphasizes national educational policies (government/policy makers), the rights of ethnic groups (learners/students) to preserve language, and community input (parents/communities) in language decisions. 'Teachers and educators' is excluded as the focus is on policy and stakeholders rather than teaching methods.

TITLE: LA CONTRIBUTION DE L'’EDUCATION AU DEVELOPPEMENT CULTUREL
paragraph: 15. Teaching history: This teaching must provide each individual with a critical knowledge of their own culture while also enabling them to know and appreciate other contributions to national and global civilization. History education should include elements related to the development of culture, thought, and universal creativity and avoid presenting history solely in terms of power struggles and conflicts. The study of religions in a spirit of tolerance could constitute one aspect of the study of cultural creation. Drawing on research results, textbook authors and history teachers should strive for the highest intellectual rigor when imparting historical knowledge to students and ensure that clichés which have fueled conflicts between human communities are not perpetuated. In the teaching of different disciplines, appropriate attention must be given to the history of the development of science and technology within the broader context of social and cultural development.

low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph focuses on history education's role in fostering cultural understanding and critical thinking, emphasizing intellectual rigor and avoiding conflict-driven narratives. This aligns with 'General (academic)' as it addresses academic content and pedagogical approaches rather than vocational skills. 'Combined general and vocational' is less fitting as there's no mention of technical training or TVET.
low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph focuses on content (history, culture, science) and learning areas (tolerance, critical knowledge) directly under 'Content and learning areas'. 'Curriculum implementation' is supported by references to teaching methods, teacher responsibilities, and avoiding clichés. 'Design and development' is excluded as the text does not discuss curriculum design processes. 'Evaluation and reform' is not explicitly mentioned.

TITLE: LA CONTRIBUTION DE L'’EDUCATION AU DEVELOPPEMENT CULTUREL
paragraph: 16. Commemorations of historical events: Commemorations such as the Fifth Centenary of the Encounter of Two Worlds (1992), the fortieth anniversary of the Bandung Conference (1995), the fiftieth anniversary of the signing of the United Nations Charter (1995), and the fiftieth anniversary of the adoption by the United Nations of the Universal Declaration of Human Rights (1998) should provide an opportunity for teachers to encourage their students to reflect on mutual enrichment, interaction, and complementarity of cultures, as well as on the contribution of all peoples to world civilization.

low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes cultural reflection, mutual enrichment, and historical commemoration, aligning with global citizenship and peace education. Human rights and equality are directly mentioned through the Universal Declaration of Human Rights reference. Other categories like citizenship and civic education are less directly supported, while digital/financial literacy and others are irrelevant.
low_level_thinking.Teachers: The paragraph emphasizes teachers' role in facilitating cultural reflection during historical commemorations, aligning with 'Teacher other' which covers unspecified teacher-related roles. Other categories like 'Professional development' or 'Curriculum' are not explicitly mentioned. 'Teacher other' is the most fitting as it captures the contextual framing of teachers' responsibilities without requiring specific training or policy details.
low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph emphasizes teachers' role in facilitating reflection on cultural interactions and historical commemorations, aligning with 'Curriculum implementation' (teacher actions). It also focuses on learning outcomes (students reflecting on cultural contributions), fitting 'Competency-based and learning outcomes'. 'Content and learning areas' was excluded as the focus is on pedagogical application, not content itself.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes curriculum-based reflection on cultural interactions and historical events, aligning with 'Curriculum and pedagogy policies'. 'Inclusion and diversity' is supported by the focus on mutual enrichment of cultures. 'International cooperation and partnerships' is implied through commemorative events involving multiple nations. 'Teachers and workforce policies' is related but not directly addressed, while 'Policy theme' is too broad.

TITLE: LA CONTRIBUTION DE L'’EDUCATION AU DEVELOPPEMENT CULTUREL
paragraph: 17. Preschool education: Given the decisive importance of the early years in the comprehensive formation of the human being, where cultural and moral aspects are essential, the network of preschool education institutions should be expanded by ensuring initial and ongoing training for teachers and equipping them with well-trained pedagogues, psychologists, and sociocultural facilitators. To take into account the children’s environment, schools are also called upon to coordinate their actions with those of families, assisting them in adopting better educational practices for their children through a well-defined lifelong education policy. At school and within the family environment, the utmost attention should be paid to health education.

low_level_thinking.Education level: The paragraph explicitly mentions 'preschool education' and 'early years' which align with ISCED 01 and 02. 'Early childhood educational development' (ISCED 01) covers the holistic development aspect, while 'Pre-primary education' (ISCED 02) directly matches the preschool focus. Categories like 'Early childhood care and education unspecified' (ISCED 0) were excluded due to the specific mention of educational institutions and pedagogical training.
low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph emphasizes expanding preschool education infrastructure (Design and development), coordinating with families and implementing lifelong education policies (Curriculum implementation), and focuses on training teachers in specific roles to enhance learning outcomes (Competency-based and learning outcomes). 'Content and learning areas' is excluded as the text does not specify content themes, and 'evaluation' is not mentioned.
low_level_thinking.Pedagogy & assessment: The paragraph emphasizes pedagogical foundations by highlighting the importance of cultural/moral aspects and interdisciplinary roles (pedagogues, psychologists). 'Professional learning communities' is supported by mentions of teacher training and ongoing education. 'Other pedagogy related issue' covers systemic coordination with families and lifelong education policy. 'Teaching methods' and 'assessment' are not explicitly addressed, while 'technology' and 'infrastructure' are not mentioned.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes quality through teacher training and pedagogical support (Quality), highlights teacher qualifications and workforce development (Teachers), mentions lifelong education policy (Lifelong Learning), and includes health education (Health). Excluded categories like Governance or Financing are not explicitly addressed.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph emphasizes teacher training and qualifications (Teachers and educators), family coordination and educational practices (Parents and communities), and systemic policy frameworks (Government and policy makers). Civil society/NGOs and private sector are not explicitly mentioned, while 'lifelong education policy' ties to government responsibility.

TITLE: LA CONTRIBUTION DE L'’EDUCATION AU DEVELOPPEMENT CULTUREL
paragraph: 18. Aesthetic and artistic education: This education should aim not only at the development of personality and openness to other sensibilities but also at fostering aesthetic and artistic appreciation as well as stimulating creativity. It is necessary to intensify this education at various levels of study, highlight its cognitive effects, notably through an interdisciplinary approach, and restore its academic importance. Artistic education must facilitate access to different cultural expressions, whether local, national, regional, or universal, in order to appreciate the diversity of values and meanings they convey. It could serve as a stage for further training in artistic professions. It is important to provide young people with opportunities to actively participate in artistic and cultural activities and to become familiar with these fields. Adequate training of teachers in aesthetic and artistic disciplines must also be organized. Cultural institutions and non-governmental organizations specializing in culture should make their resources as widely available as possible to schools and communities to thereby strengthen their educational functions.

low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes aesthetic and artistic education as a core academic discipline with cognitive effects and interdisciplinary approaches, aligning with 'General (academic)'. Vocational/technical categories are excluded as the focus is on cultural appreciation and holistic development, not job-specific skills.
low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph emphasizes content related to aesthetic/artistic education (Content and learning areas), outlines implementation strategies like interdisciplinary approaches and teacher training (Curriculum implementation), mentions learning outcomes such as creativity and cultural appreciation (Competency-based and learning outcomes), and includes resource availability for institutions (Other curriculum-related issue). 'Organization and instructional time' is excluded as the text does not specify time allocation. 'Curriculum evaluation and reform' is not directly addressed.
low_level_thinking.Pedagogy & assessment: The paragraph emphasizes pedagogical foundations (interdisciplinary approaches, cognitive effects), teaching methods (interdisciplinary, cultural participation), professional development (teacher training), and other pedagogy issues (resource availability, institutional roles). 'Assessment and feedback' is excluded as no explicit assessment mechanisms are mentioned.
low_level_thinking.Subject domain: The paragraph explicitly focuses on aesthetic and artistic education, aligning with 'Arts and culture'. The interdisciplinary approach and cognitive effects link to 'Social sciences and humanities'. Other categories like 'Education orientation' or 'Pedagogy & assessment' are not allowed, and 'Infrastructure & resources' is excluded as the focus is on education content, not physical resources.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes creativity and interdisciplinary approaches, aligning with life skills. It mentions access to diverse cultural expressions, supporting inclusive education.
low_level_thinking.Ownership/Provision: The paragraph emphasizes state-run institutions (e.g., 'schools and communities') and public-private partnerships through cultural institutions and NGOs. 'Community or faith-based' is supported by references to community engagement and cultural institutions. 'Public' aligns with state-run education systems. Private sectors are not explicitly mentioned, so 'Private non-profit' and 'Private for-profit' are excluded.
low_level_thinking.Infrastructure & resources: The paragraph emphasizes providing resources (e.g., 'cultural institutions...make their resources as widely available') aligning with 'Infrastructure & resources'. 'Teaching and learning materials' fits due to mentions of 'adequate training of teachers' and 'artistic education' requiring materials. Other categories like 'School buildings' or 'ICT' are not explicitly mentioned, and 'WASH' is irrelevant.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph emphasizes teacher training (Teachers and educators), cultural institutions/NGOs providing resources (Civil society and NGOs), and community engagement through cultural activities (Parents and communities). Policy makers and private sector are not explicitly mentioned, while learners are implied but not directly labeled.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes curriculum development (interdisciplinary approach, cognitive effects) and pedagogy (teaching methods, active participation). Inclusion and diversity are addressed through cultural expressions and accessibility. Teachers' training is explicitly mentioned, and resource mobilization is implied via cultural institutions' role. Financing is indirectly referenced through resource availability.

TITLE: LA CONTRIBUTION DE L'’EDUCATION AU DEVELOPPEMENT CULTUREL
paragraph: 19. Development of ethical and civic values: The educational system must assume a fundamental role in ethical, civic, and moral formation, complementing the role played by families. Alongside other institutions, it should contribute to the promotion of human rights, the development of democratic behaviors, and the affirmation of values that are more urgently needed today than ever before, such as respect for human dignity, tolerance, dialogue, solidarity, and mutual assistance. To develop these qualities, teachers could engage with their students in solidarity and mutual aid activities in support of the sick, the disadvantaged or socially deprived, the elderly, and victims of disasters; students could also act as facilitators in educational, sports, and other activities aimed at younger students and out-of-school children. It is important to provide learners with examples of moral behavior aligned with the values enumerated above. In the teaching of science and technology, special attention will be given to ethical aspects and behaviors.

low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes ethical and civic formation, aligning with 'Citizenship and civic education'. Human rights and values like tolerance are explicitly mentioned, supporting 'Human rights and equality'. 'Life skills and 21st century competencies' fits solidarity, dialogue, and mutual aid activities. 'Global citizenship' is excluded as the focus is on local civic roles, not global perspectives. 'Digital literacy' is not mentioned, and 'inclusive education' is not the primary theme.
low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph emphasizes content related to ethical and civic values (Content and learning areas), outlines specific learning outcomes like promoting human rights and solidarity (Competency-based and learning outcomes), and describes how these values should be integrated into teaching practices (Curriculum implementation). 'Design and development' and 'Curriculum evaluation and reform' are less directly supported, while 'Other curriculum-related issue' is too vague.
low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes ethical and civic values as core educational components, aligning with 'General (academic)' through curriculum integration. 'Combined general and vocational' is supported by mentions of science/technology education with ethical focus and practical activities involving students in solidarity efforts. 'Vocational/technical (TVET)' is excluded as the primary focus is on values and pedagogy, not specific technical skills.
low_level_thinking.Teachers: The paragraph emphasizes ethical and civic education as part of the curriculum, aligning with 'Initial education and training'. It also discusses teachers' roles in facilitating activities and modeling moral behavior, fitting 'Teacher other'. Categories like 'Professional development' and 'Curriculum' were considered but not explicitly addressed. 'Subject domain' was debated but not directly applicable.

TITLE: LA CONTRIBUTION DE L'’EDUCATION AU DEVELOPPEMENT CULTUREL
paragraph: 20. Introduction to major contemporary world issues: In order to enhance learners’ understanding of the political, social, cultural, economic, demographic, environmental, and other problems faced by contemporary societies and to better prepare them to address these issues, teachers must be trained to handle and provide information within the teaching process and to explain its historical background, evolution, and consequences, while assisting learners, both young and adult, within formal and non-formal educational structures, to form their own personal opinions. It is important that each learner does not remain indifferent to events occurring beyond their own country or region but develops a spirit of solidarity and shared responsibility toward common global problems. The media can be useful tools to achieve this objective.

low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph focuses on teacher training and curriculum integration to enhance global awareness and critical thinking, aligning with 'General (academic)'. 'Combined general and vocational' is excluded as there's no vocational/technical focus. 'Vocational/technical (TVET)' is not applicable here.
low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph emphasizes content areas (global issues, historical context) and learning outcomes (forming personal opinions, global solidarity). 'Curriculum implementation' is supported by mentions of teacher training and instructional methods. 'Competency-based' aligns with developing skills for addressing global problems. 'Design and development' was excluded as the focus is on implementation, not design. 'Organization and instructional time' lacks direct mention. 'Evaluation and reform' is not addressed.
low_level_thinking.Teachers: The paragraph emphasizes training teachers to address global issues, aligning with 'Initial education and training' and 'Professional development'. 'Teacher other' was excluded as the focus is on training, not broader teacher roles. 'Recruitment and hiring' and 'Qualification and certification' were not explicitly mentioned.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes global solidarity, shared responsibility, and teacher training to address cross-cutting issues like political, social, and environmental challenges, aligning with 'Global citizenship and peace education' and 'Citizenship and civic education'. 'Other cross-cutting theme' was excluded due to lack of specific mention, while categories like digital literacy or sustainable development were not directly referenced.
low_level_thinking.Subject domain: The paragraph emphasizes global issues including political, social, cultural, economic, demographic, and environmental problems, aligning with 'Social sciences and humanities'. Environmental issues are explicitly mentioned, supporting 'Environmental education and sustainable development'. Other categories like 'Other subject area' were excluded as the focus remains within established domains.
low_level_thinking.Learner population: The paragraph explicitly mentions 'learners, both young and adult,' directly supporting 'Youth and adolescents' and 'Adults and lifelong learners.' Other categories like 'Children (0-17)' are not explicitly named, and no evidence exists for marginalized groups or specific demographics. The summary confirms 'Learner population' includes diverse learners, aligning with the dual reference to young and adult learners.

TITLE: LA CONTRIBUTION DE L'’EDUCATION AU DEVELOPPEMENT CULTUREL
paragraph: 21. Development of attitudes favorable to environmental protection: The formal and non-formal educational process should raise awareness among young people and adults about environmental issues and the increasing deterioration of the natural and cultural environment. The educational system must also foster a sense of responsibility in learners and develop in them a new behavioral model aimed at enabling them to contribute individually to the preservation and improvement of the environment. It should also sensitize them to certain issues such as the production, treatment, and transfer of toxic waste, as well as their ecological and cultural consequences.

low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes raising awareness and fostering responsibility through education, which aligns with general academic orientation. Vocational/technical categories focus on job-specific skills, not addressed here. Combined categories imply both general and vocational elements, which are not present.
low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph focuses on designing educational processes to foster environmental awareness and responsibility, aligning with 'Design and development'. It specifies content areas like toxic waste and ecological consequences, fitting 'Content and learning areas'. 'Curriculum implementation' and 'evaluation' are not explicitly addressed. 'Competency-based' is implied but not directly stated. 'Other' is avoided due to specificity.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes environmental protection, toxic waste management, and behavioral change, aligning with 'Sustainable development and climate action'. It also mentions developing responsibility, new behavioral models, and awareness, which fit 'Life skills and 21st century competencies'. Other categories like 'Citizenship' or 'Human rights' are not explicitly addressed here.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes curriculum-based education (Curriculum and pedagogy policies) and environmental awareness (Sustainable development and environment). Lifelong learning is implied through references to both young people and adults. Governance and planning, financing, and other categories lack direct evidence. 'Education orientation' is not explicitly mentioned, and 'cross-cutting themes' are not framed as policy themes here.

TITLE: LA CONTRIBUTION DE L'’EDUCATION AU DEVELOPPEMENT CULTUREL
paragraph: 22. Access to education and cultural life for disadvantaged or disabled persons: Public authorities and other responsible bodies must take the necessary measures to ensure access to education and cultural life, and guarantee equal opportunities for persons who are socially or economically disadvantaged, the elderly, as well as those with physical or mental disabilities. Social and physical barriers, as well as attitudes that hinder full participation on an equal footing in educational and cultural programs, must be eliminated.

low_level_thinking.Learner population: The paragraph explicitly mentions 'disadvantaged or disabled persons' and 'socially or economically disadvantaged' groups, directly aligning with 'Low-income and disadvantaged groups' and 'Learners with disabilities'. 'Learner population' is the high-level label, so it is included. Other categories like 'Policy theme' or 'Legal frameworks' are excluded as per the summary's consensus to focus on population categories.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes access and equity for disadvantaged/disabled groups, aligning with 'Access and equity'. 'Inclusion and diversity' matches the focus on eliminating barriers for marginalized populations. 'Policy theme' is explicitly stated in the orchestrator summary. Excluded categories like 'Legal frameworks' and 'Cross-cutting themes' were discussed but not directly referenced in the paragraph's normative language.
low_level_thinking.Legal frameworks: The paragraph mandates measures and equal opportunities, aligning with education laws and regulations. It outlines obligations for public authorities, fitting 'Rights and obligations of stakeholders'. 'Policy implementation and enforcement' matches the requirement to eliminate barriers. 'Legal frameworks' is excluded as the focus is on specific policy measures, not broader legal structures.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph explicitly mentions equal opportunities and elimination of barriers for disadvantaged/disabled individuals, aligning with 'Human rights and equality' and 'Inclusive education and special needs'. 'Other cross-cutting theme' is included as the focus on access and barrier elimination fits broader cross-cutting themes beyond specific categories. 'Policy theme' and 'Legal frameworks' from the summary are excluded as the paragraph focuses on implementation rather than policy or legal mandates.

TITLE: LA CONTRIBUTION DE L'’EDUCATION AU DEVELOPPEMENT CULTUREL
paragraph: 23. Cultural development through the interaction of the school, the community, and society: As part of the local community, the school should, on the one hand, prepare students to participate in the socio-economic and cultural life of the community and, on the other hand, serve as a center for education and cultural activities. To make the school an effective instrument of community cohesion, especially in rural areas, it is necessary to establish consultation and cooperation between educational authorities and all partners: parents, authorities, local associations and businesses, as well as cultural institutions, which should assist schools in strengthening their cultural engagement with the community.

low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes cultural development and community engagement through education, aligning with 'General (academic)' as it focuses on broader educational roles rather than specific vocational skills. 'Combined general and vocational' is excluded because the text does not mention technical or vocational training. 'Vocational/technical (TVET)' is also excluded as there is no reference to job-specific skills or technical education.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph emphasizes consultation and cooperation with parents, local associations, businesses, and cultural institutions, aligning with 'Parents and communities', 'Civil society and NGOs', and 'Private sector and industry'. 'Government and policy makers' is excluded as the focus is on collaboration rather than direct government action.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes community engagement, cultural development, and collaboration (indicating 'Other cross-cutting theme'). 'Life skills and 21st century competencies' aligns with preparing students for socio-economic participation. 'Inclusive education and special needs' is implied through the mention of diverse community partners. Categories like citizenship or digital literacy are not explicitly addressed.

TITLE: LA CONTRIBUTION DE L'’EDUCATION AU DEVELOPPEMENT CULTUREL
paragraph: 24. Role of the educational system with regard to women and girls: Equal opportunities for girls and boys in access to education, continuation of their studies, and their academic and professional orientations should be made effective. Women play a crucial role in the transmission of cultural and moral values. In adult education, the educational system must find ways to organize literacy, general culture, pedagogy, civic education, health education, and sports activities for both sexes; these activities should have the effect, among other things, of abandoning certain traditional practices that harm the health and dignity of women and girls.

low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes academic access and continuation (General (academic)) and mentions civic education, health education, and sports activities which blend general and vocational elements (Combined general and vocational). Vocational/technical (TVET) is excluded as the focus is not on specific technical skills.
low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph discusses specific learning areas like literacy, general culture, pedagogy, civic education, health education, and sports activities, supporting 'Content and learning areas'. It also mentions organizing these activities within the educational system, indicating 'Curriculum implementation'. 'Design and development' is less applicable as the focus is on implementation rather than design. 'Curriculum evaluation and reform' is not explicitly mentioned.

paragraph: 25. Educational role of the media: The ever-increasing role of the media in human life necessitates both the expansion and improvement of their use for educational purposes. To this end, media managers, whether public or private, are invited, in collaboration with educational and cultural institutions, to: (a) increase the quantity and diversity of their educational and cultural programs aimed at different categories of learners and the general population; ensure these programs maintain adequate pedagogical quality; (b) provide schools and non-formal education centers with the necessary teaching materials and other resources to promote media education.

Educational authorities should strengthen their support for these initiatives. The outcomes of this collaboration should be disseminated throughout the teaching community to enhance the multiplier effect of pioneering projects.

TITLE: LA CONTRIBUTION DE L'’EDUCATION AU DEVELOPPEMENT CULTUREL
paragraph: 28. Role and training of teachers: The teacher plays a unique and essential role in promoting the contribution of education to cultural development. In this regard, it is indispensable that the teacher genuinely understands the significance of their mission. Their services must therefore be recognized and valued by the community. The cultural dimension must be taken into account in the initial and ongoing training of all teachers. One of the objectives of this training should be to provide them with a broader and deeper knowledge of cultures in their diverse expressions within the country and around the world. Teacher training should also take into consideration the importance of media education, as well as technological instruction and environmental education. Furthermore, it should equip them with the necessary skills to integrate students from non-dominant cultures into the learning process. Intercultural and/or multicultural education requires specific competencies related to its innovative nature. It involves the implementation of adapted pedagogies that must intelligently and tactfully account for the heterogeneity of the audiences addressed. It also demands appropriate evaluation systems. Mastery of methods and techniques of observation, listening, and intercultural communication, proficiency in several working languages where appropriate, and a basic understanding of anthropological analysis will enable teachers to better fulfill their professional duties within the complex reality of cultural plurality. This training must be supported by centers of pedagogical and cultural research, cultural institutions, higher education establishments, and the media. It will rely, whenever possible, on local cultural resources.

paragraph: 30. Actions for the promotion of intercultural/multicultural education: For a better mutual understanding of diverse cultures, it is essential that national, regional, and international bodies encourage between countries: (a) exchanges of pupils, students, children, youth, teachers, youth workers, researchers, and specialists in cultural matters, including training programs abroad; (b) exchanges between schools and youth organizations; (c) the learning of modern foreign languages; (d) the exchange of teaching and audiovisual materials, scientific equipment, and research programs in education; (e) the promotion of educational and cultural programs of common interest. To promote these activities, the aforementioned authorities could utilize UNESCO networks.

paragraph: 32. Recommends that UNESCO, in cooperation with the relevant intergovernmental and non-governmental organizations, and with a view to ensuring a greater emphasis on the cultural and intercultural aspects of education: (i) strengthen, through an intersectoral and multidisciplinary approach, projects and programs falling within these areas; and accord priority in the next Medium-Term Plan to the role of education in cultural development; (ii) encourage the collection, analysis, and dissemination of information and experiences concerning innovations in cultural and intercultural education, notably those of the Associated Schools Network; (iii) stimulate the definition of quality criteria regarding intercultural/multicultural education; (iv) support the efforts of national education systems to promote intercultural education and related research; also support the study, production, and dissemination of teaching materials for intercultural education, particularly history textbooks (including the history of science and technology), as well as teacher training; (vi) draw the attention of the Intergovernmental Committee for the World Decade for Cultural Development, the World Commission on Culture and Development, and the International Commission on Education for the Twenty-first Century to the reflections on the contribution of education to cultural development carried out during this 43rd session of the International Conference on Education.

TITLE: l'éducation pour la paix, les droits de l'homme et la démocratie, Reconnaissant tout l’intérêt de la Recommandation de 1974, compte tenu de la mission éthique, éducative et intellectuelle de l'UNESCO, Prenant en considération le fait que, dans leurs réponses au questionnaire du BIE relatif à l’évaluation de la mise en oeuvre et à l’éventuelle actualisation de la Recommandation de 1974, les Etats membres ont souligné le rôle important joué par la Recommandation pour la promotion de l'éducation, Consciente en même temps que, comme l’ont indiqué les Etats membres ainsi que les experts des réunions préparatoires régionales, les récents changements intervenus aux niveaux international et national et les tendances et les besoins nouveaux des sociétés et de l'humanité dans son ensemble font que le rôle de l'éducation est plus crucial que jamais pour promouvoir une culture de la paix, des droits de l’homme et de la démocratie, nécessitant l’examen d’une éventuelle actualisation de la Recommandation de 1974, adoptée dans un contexte socio¬ historique différent, Considérant que la Déclaration et le Cadre d’action intégré de

paragraph: 2.3.3 develop in teachers the fundamental skills necessary for fulfilling their irreplaceable role, which consists, on the one hand, in organizing information so that it becomes knowledge, making choices, and fostering critical thinking, and on the other hand, transmitting culture, establishing personal and social relationships, reaching out to others, prioritizing communal life, and fostering awareness of differences and shared values. These competencies particularly concern: in-service training: mastery of concepts and knowledge related to the subject taught, as well as pedagogical skills and the integration of attitudes necessary for the continuous updating of their professional qualifications, enabling them to adapt to the evolving process of knowledge and the diversity of educational challenges to be addressed; teamwork: fostering attitudes conducive to dialogue and cooperation with colleagues and other educational staff at all levels, essential conditions for collective professionalism that defines the teaching profession; innovation and experimentation: development of scientific thinking, basic training in the methods of educational research, and active participation in the evaluation of experiments; respect for others, human rights, peace, and democracy: personal commitment and professional capacities to teach students to live together, to prevent and resolve conflicts through dialogue and negotiation, and to reject violence and intolerance towards others; cultural diversity: consideration of cultural diversity, essential for mutual understanding in an intercultural/multicultural environment; respect for nature: increased awareness of the challenges facing our environment and acquisition of professional skills enabling students to act both personally and collectively to protect it.

TITLE: LES ENSEIGNANTS ET LEURS PARTENAIRES DANS LE PROCESSUS EDUCATIF: L’EDUCATION, RESPONSABILITE DE TOUS
paragraph: 5.1 Since the Jomtien Conference, there has been a general consensus to recognize that education is the responsibility of all, not solely of governments or teachers. Partnership has become a necessary condition for the transformation of education in a world where knowledge and information are central elements of the economy, and where respect for cultural diversity, gender equality, and other human rights is crucial for democratic stability. Certainly, the forms of partnership vary according to cultural, political, and administrative traditions, but regional discussions have identified two main courses of action: opening schools to the needs and demands of the community and, on the other hand, enabling schools to participate in community development activities. However, partnership experiences show that this is not a simple or easy course of action: "Partnership succeeds or fails: partners either agree or disagree. The path of partnership is not without difficulties, but it provides a viable solution compared to the isolationist option." Partnership must be conceived as a factor for improving the quality of education; it must therefore be perceived not as an end in itself, nor as a source of abdication of responsibilities by the State, teachers, or even the community, but rather as an effective form of stakeholder participation and the exercise of shared responsibilities.

low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph emphasizes shared responsibilities among governments (as primary providers), teachers (as key stakeholders), and communities (including parents) in education partnerships. 'Parents and communities' is explicitly mentioned as having needs and demands, while 'Government and policy makers' and 'Teachers and educators' are implied through references to state responsibilities and professional roles. 'Civil society and NGOs' and 'Private sector and industry' are not directly referenced.
low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes partnership in education as a shared responsibility involving stakeholders, aligning with 'General (academic)' which covers broad educational principles. 'Vocational/technical (TVET)' and 'Combined general and vocational' are excluded as the text focuses on stakeholder collaboration and quality improvement, not specific skills or TVET.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes partnership as a key factor for education transformation, aligning with 'International cooperation and partnerships'. It also discusses shared responsibilities and stakeholder participation, fitting 'Governance and planning'. Other categories like 'Access and equity' or 'Quality and learning outcomes' are not explicitly addressed.

paragraph: 6.1 The new information and communication technologies are one of the central elements of this changing world. The report of the International Commission on Education for the Twenty-first Century clearly highlights the stakes of the impact of new technologies: "By abolishing distance, they powerfully contribute to shaping the societies of tomorrow, which will not conform, because of them, to any model of the past... The most precise and up-to-date information can be made available to anyone on the surface of the globe" (but) "It must not be forgotten, however, that a still very large disadvantaged population remains excluded from this evolution.

Learner population.category: Population in conflict and emergencies settings

paragraph: 7.2 Although national or regional situations vary greatly, the enhancement of teachers’ status often appears as a necessary condition for strengthening their role. However, this enhancement cannot be the result of a single measure or factor. In this respect, improving the material situation of teachers, particularly their salary and other social benefits, is a necessary but not sufficient condition for the enhancement of their status: it is the entire set of educational factors that must be improved. In an integrated policy for the enhancement of teachers’ status, professionalization constitutes the most promising strategy in the medium and long term. In addition to the recommendations made regarding their recruitment, training, participation in management, and autonomy in pedagogical decision-making, the enhancement of teachers’ status can proceed according to the following lines of action:

TITLE: SOLIDARITE AVEC LES ENSEIGNANTS QUI TRAVAILLENT DANS DES SITUATIONS DIFFICILES
paragraph: 8.1 In many countries, teachers are called upon to carry out their profession with specific populations (people without resources, marginalized groups, refugees, migrants, street children, offenders, women from certain traditional societies, etc.) or to work under particularly difficult conditions (remote, mountainous, or rural regions, multi-grade or overcrowded classes, etc.). Armed conflicts, very often with cross-border dimensions, constitute a particularly concerning situation. Children are always the most traumatized victims of these conflicts, whose psychological and moral consequences will follow them throughout their lives. Whatever the situation, whether it involves special population groups, hard-to-reach areas, or zones affected by armed conflicts, an integrated and priority strategy should be adopted to address the concrete pedagogical specificities. Although variable in scale and nature, all these situations require from the teacher, more than elsewhere, the mobilization of all their emotional and psychological capacities, diversified skills—in short, true versatility. Yet, as has been observed, teachers are little or not at all prepared for life and work in difficult contexts.

Learner population.category: Population in conflict and emergencies settings

paragraph: C. Conclusions of the major debates The Conference considered that the two major plenary debates: “Teachers in search of new perspectives” and “The role of teachers in building a culture of peace,” made it possible to identify a number of key elements. These are part of the follow-up to the Declaration of the 44th session of the International Conference on Education. They enrich the Declaration and recommendations of this 45th session of the International Conference on Education with a series of conclusions. These respond to society’s expectations regarding its teachers and the major role education must play as a national priority in promoting socio-economic development, peace, justice, and democratic principles.

paragraph: Given the enormous complexity of the challenges faced by all societies, notably globalization and intolerable inequalities between and within countries, learning to live together, a concept developed by the International Commission on Education for the Twenty-first Century, is an imperative in all regions of the world.

'Learning to live together' also matches life skills and 21st century competencies as it involves social and collaborative competencies. Other categories like inclusive education or digital literacy are not explicitly mentioned.
low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes 'learning to live together' as a conceptual imperative for global challenges, aligning with general academic education orientations. Vocational/technical categories are excluded as the focus is on broad educational purpose rather than specific skills. Combined categories are not selected due to lack of explicit vocational references.

TITLE: PROPOSITIONS D'ACTION PROPOSITIONS D'ACTION ISSUES DE LA 46e SESSION DE LA CIE [ADDED]
paragraph: One of the major challenges facing educational systems remains ensuring and respecting the right to education for all. TITLE: PROPOSITIONS D'ACTION PROPOSITIONS D'ACTION ISSUES DE LA 46e SESSION DE LA CIE [ADDED]
paragraph: The achievement of the goal of education for all goes beyond the effort of universal schooling.
